daily.label: The Baseline Protocol
daily.subtitle: Spine, Core, Posterior
daily.purpose: Spine, core, and posterior chain. The foundation session.

sections.label: Warm-up & Mobility
sections.purpose: Prepare the spine and joints for load. These movements are not a formality. They set the neurological tone for everything that follows. Move slowly and with full attention.

exercises.name: Cat–Cow
exercises.start: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.

steps.label: Neutral Tabletop
steps.cue: Inhale — let your belly drop toward the floor. Lift your gaze and tailbone gently.

steps.label: Cat — Exhale
steps.cue: Exhale — round your spine toward the ceiling. Tuck your chin to your chest and pelvis under.

steps.label: Cow — Inhale
steps.cue: Move with your breath. Don't force the lower back on the inhale.

exercises.position: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.
exercises.next: Place your hands under your shoulders and your knees under your hips. Keep your back flat.
exercises.then: Drop your belly toward the floor. Lift your head and tailbone up. Breathe in.
exercises.tip: Push your back up toward the ceiling. Tuck your chin to your chest. Breathe out.

exercises.name: Sphinx Pose
exercises.start: Lie flat on your stomach with your legs straight.

steps.label: Prone Start
steps.cue: Bring your forearms to the floor with elbows directly under your shoulders.

steps.label: Lift
steps.cue: Press forearms down to lift your chest. Keep hips on the floor. Hold for the full duration.
This is a passive hold. Let the spine decompress — don't force the lift.

exercises.position: Lie flat on your stomach with your legs straight.
exercises.next: Place your forearms flat on the floor. Keep your elbows under your shoulders.
exercises.then: Press your forearms into the floor to lift your chest. Keep your hips flat on the floor.
exercises.tip: Let your lower back relax. Do not push your chest too high.

exercises.name: Child's Pose
exercises.start: Kneel on the floor. Keep your big toes together and spread your knees wide.

steps.label: Kneel Upright
steps.cue: Fold forward and rest your forehead on the floor. Reach arms straight out in front.

steps.label: Fold Forward
steps.cue: Breathe deeply into your lower back. Let your chest sink toward the floor. Hold.
Widen your knees if you need more room for your torso.

exercises.position: Kneel on the floor. Keep your big toes together and spread your knees wide.
exercises.next: Sit your hips back onto your heels.
exercises.then: Reach your arms straight out in front of you. Rest your forehead on the floor.
exercises.tip: Take deep breaths into your lower back. Hold.

exercises.name: Arm Circles - Right Arm
exercises.start: Stand tall with your feet apart.

steps.label: Stand Tall
steps.cue: Make small controlled circles moving forward. Gradually increase to full arm sweeps. Keep shoulders down.

steps.label: Forward Circles
steps.cue: Reverse direction. Same progression, small to large. Slight squeeze of shoulder blades on the back swing.

steps.label: Backward Circles
steps.cue: Keep shoulders away from your ears throughout both directions.

exercises.position: Stand tall with your feet apart.
exercises.next: Reach your right arm straight out to the side at shoulder height.
exercises.then: Move your right arm in slow circles forward. Make the circles bigger as you go. Do 10 times.
exercises.tip: Switch directions. Move your arm in slow circles backward 10 times.

exercises.name: Arm Circles - Left Arm
exercises.start: Stand tall with your feet apart.

steps.label: Stand Tall
steps.cue: Make small controlled circles moving forward. Gradually increase to full arm sweeps. Keep shoulders down.

steps.label: Forward Circles
steps.cue: Reverse direction. Same progression, small to large. Slight squeeze of shoulder blades on the back swing.

steps.label: Backward Circles
steps.cue: Keep shoulders away from your ears throughout both directions.

exercises.position: Stand tall with your feet apart.
exercises.next: Reach your left arm straight out to the side at shoulder height.
exercises.then: Move your left arm in slow circles forward. Make the circles bigger as you go. Do 10 times.
exercises.tip: Switch directions. Move your arm in slow circles backward 10 times.

sections.label: Core & Posterior Chain
sections.purpose: Build the deep stability that protects the spine under load. Quality over quantity. Slow, controlled reps with full awareness of position do more than fast repetitions ever will.

exercises.name: Pelvic Tilts
exercises.start: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.

steps.label: Supine Neutral
steps.cue: Gently tighten your abs and press your lower back flat into the floor. Hold 2 seconds.

steps.label: Posterior Tilt
steps.cue: Relax and let the lower back arch slightly away from the floor. Then press flat again. Repeat slowly.

steps.label: Anterior Tilt
steps.cue: The movement is small and subtle. Do not hold your breath.

exercises.position: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Keep a small gap between your lower back and the floor. Rest your arms at your sides.
exercises.then: Squeeze your stomach muscles and press your lower back flat against the floor. Hold for 2 seconds.
exercises.tip: Relax your stomach muscles and let your lower back lift again. Move slowly.

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
exercises.start: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.

steps.label: Supine Setup
steps.cue: Press through your heels and lift your hips until shoulders, hips, and knees form a straight line.

steps.label: Bridge Up
steps.cue: Squeeze your glutes at the top. Hold briefly, then lower slowly back to the floor.
Do not let your lower back arch at the top. Keep your core tight throughout.

exercises.position: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Keep your feet hip-width apart. Rest your arms flat at your sides.
exercises.then: Push your heels into the floor. Lift your hips until your body makes a straight line from your shoulders to your knees. Squeeze your butt at the top.
exercises.tip: Lower your hips slowly back to the floor.

exercises.name: Marching Bridge
exercises.start: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.

steps.label: Hold Bridge
steps.cue: Keeping hips level, slowly lift one foot off the floor and bring your knee to 90 degrees. Hold 2 seconds.

steps.label: Lift One Foot
steps.cue: Lower that foot, then lift the other. Alternate slowly without letting your hips drop or rotate.

steps.label: Alternate
steps.cue: Think of balancing a glass of water on your hips. Keep it level throughout.

exercises.position: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Push your heels into the floor and lift your hips up. Keep your hips lifted.
exercises.then: Lift your right foot off the floor and pull your right knee toward your chest. Hold for 2 seconds.
exercises.tip: Lower your right foot. Lift your left foot. Keep your hips completely still.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.start: Lie on your back.

steps.label: Supine Tabletop
steps.cue: Slowly lower your right arm overhead and extend your left leg toward the floor simultaneously. Keep your lower back pressed into the floor.

steps.label: Extend Opposite Limbs
steps.cue: Return to start. Repeat with your left arm and right leg. Move in slow 4-second counts each way.

steps.label: Return & Switch
steps.cue: Opposite arm and leg always move together. Control matters more than range.

exercises.position: Lie on your back.
exercises.next: Reach your arms straight up to the ceiling. Lift your feet off the floor and bend your knees.
exercises.then: Reach your right arm straight back over your head. Straighten your left leg down toward the floor at the same time.
exercises.tip: Bring your arm and leg back up. Switch sides. Move very slowly.

exercises.name: Bird Dog
exercises.start: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.

steps.label: Neutral Tabletop
steps.cue: Reach your right arm straight forward and extend your left leg straight back. Both parallel to the floor. Hold 2 seconds.

steps.label: Extend & Pause
steps.cue: Draw elbow to knee beneath your body. Return to neutral. Switch sides and repeat.

steps.label: Return Controlled
steps.cue: Opposite arm and leg — not the same side. Keep hips level and do not rotate.

exercises.position: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.
exercises.next: Keep your back flat and look down at the floor.
exercises.then: Reach your right arm straight forward. Reach your left leg straight backward. Hold for 2 seconds.
exercises.tip: Lower your hand and knee back to the floor. Switch sides.

sections.label: Upper Body
sections.purpose: Restore strength and control to the shoulder girdle and chest. These movements rebuild the pushing and pulling patterns that daily life often imbalances. Keep the neck and jaw soft throughout.

exercises.name: High Plank
exercises.start: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.

steps.label: Hand Position
steps.cue: Step feet back until your body forms one straight line from head to heels. Squeeze your glutes.

steps.label: Full Plank
steps.cue: Pull your navel toward your spine and breathe steadily. Hold for the full duration.
Do not let your hips sag or lift. If your wrists ache, drop to your forearms.

exercises.position: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.
exercises.next: Place your hands flat on the floor under your shoulders.
exercises.then: Step both feet backward to straighten your legs. Keep your body in a straight line from your head to your heels.
exercises.tip: Squeeze your stomach and butt muscles. Do not let your hips sag down.

exercises.name: Wall Push-Ups
exercises.start: Stand facing a wall, one arm's length away.

steps.label: Wall Setup
steps.cue: Bend your elbows and lower your chest toward the wall over 2 to 3 seconds. Keep your body in a straight line.

steps.label: Lower
steps.cue: Push through your palms to return to start. Fully extend your arms. Pause briefly at the top.

steps.label: Press Back
steps.cue: Step further from the wall to increase difficulty. No sagging hips.

exercises.position: Stand facing a wall, one arm's length away.
exercises.next: Place your hands flat on the wall at shoulder height. Keep your body straight.
exercises.then: Bend your elbows. Lean your chest toward the wall slowly.
exercises.tip: Push the wall away to straighten your arms. Step your feet backward to make it harder.

exercises.name: Isometric Wall Push Hold
exercises.start: Stand facing a wall, one arm's length away.

steps.label: Wall Setup
steps.cue: Bend your elbows to roughly 90 degrees — the midpoint of a wall push-up. Stop here.

steps.label: Halfway Hold
steps.cue: Press your hands firmly into the wall and hold completely still for the full duration. Breathe steadily.
This is a still exercise — no movement is the point. You will feel tension in your chest and arms.

exercises.position: Stand facing a wall, one arm's length away.
exercises.next: Place your hands flat on the wall at shoulder height. Step your feet slightly backward.
exercises.then: Bend your elbows halfway down. Stop and hold this position completely still.
exercises.tip: Push your hands firmly into the wall. Keep your stomach muscles tight.

exercises.name: Resistance Band Row
exercises.start: Sit on the floor with your legs straight out in front of you.

steps.label: Band Setup
steps.cue: Sit tall with slight tension in the band at arm's length. Keep your back straight.

steps.label: Row Back
steps.cue: Pull both hands toward your lower abdomen, driving elbows back and squeezing shoulder blades together. Pause 1 second, then extend slowly.

steps.label: Controlled Return
steps.cue: Do not let your shoulders roll forward at full extension.

exercises.position: Sit on the floor with your legs straight out in front of you.
exercises.next: Wrap a resistance band around the bottoms of your feet. Hold one end in each hand. Sit tall.
exercises.then: Pull both hands toward your stomach. Squeeze your shoulder blades together.
exercises.tip: Straighten your arms slowly. Keep your back straight.

exercises.name: Wall Angels
exercises.start: Stand with your back flat against a wall.

steps.label: Wall Contact
steps.cue: Slowly slide your arms up the wall, maintaining contact at all times. Go only as far as contact allows.

steps.label: Slide Up
steps.cue: Slide back down to start over 3 seconds. If contact is lost, reduce your range.

steps.label: Slide Down
steps.cue: Quality over range. Back, head, and hands all stay on the wall throughout.

exercises.position: Stand with your back flat against a wall.
exercises.next: Press your head, upper back, and hips into the wall. Bend your elbows and press your arms against the wall to make a W shape.
exercises.then: Slide your arms slowly up the wall to make a Y shape. Keep your arms touching the wall.
exercises.tip: Slide your arms slowly back down. Stop if your arms pull away from the wall.

sections.label: Lower Body & Rotation
sections.purpose: Release tension accumulated in the hips, hamstrings, and lumbar spine. Let gravity and breath do the work. These are passive holds. There is nothing to push through.

exercises.name: Supine Hamstring Stretch - Right Leg
exercises.start: Lie flat on your back with your legs straight.

steps.label: Supine Start
steps.cue: Lift one leg, keeping the knee as straight as comfortable. Hold behind the thigh or calf with both hands. No reach? Use a towel around your foot.

steps.label: Raise One Leg
steps.cue: Hold the stretch for the full duration. Feel the pull in the back of your thigh, not your knee. Switch sides when cued.
Do not lock the knee. A mild pull is correct — sharp discomfort is not.

exercises.position: Lie flat on your back with your legs straight.
exercises.next: Keep your left leg straight on the floor. Relax your shoulders.
exercises.then: Lift your right leg straight up toward the ceiling. Grab the back of your right thigh with both hands.
exercises.tip: Pull your leg gently toward your chest until you feel a stretch. Keep your knee slightly bent.

exercises.name: Supine Hamstring Stretch - Left Leg
exercises.start: Lie flat on your back with your legs straight.

steps.label: Supine Start
steps.cue: Lift one leg, keeping the knee as straight as comfortable. Hold behind the thigh or calf with both hands. No reach? Use a towel around your foot.

steps.label: Raise One Leg
steps.cue: Hold the stretch for the full duration. Feel the pull in the back of your thigh, not your knee. Switch sides when cued.
Do not lock the knee. A mild pull is correct — sharp discomfort is not.

exercises.position: Lie flat on your back with your legs straight.
exercises.next: Keep your right leg straight on the floor. Relax your shoulders.
exercises.then: Lift your left leg straight up toward the ceiling. Grab the back of your left thigh with both hands.
exercises.tip: Pull your leg gently toward your chest until you feel a stretch. Keep your knee slightly bent.

exercises.name: Figure Four Stretch - Right Hip
exercises.start: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.

steps.label: Cross Leg Over Knee
steps.cue: Lift your left foot off the floor and draw both legs toward your chest until you feel a stretch in your right hip.

steps.label: Draw In
steps.cue: Hold for the full duration. Use your hand to gently press your right knee outward to deepen the stretch. Switch sides when cued.
You should feel this in the outer hip and glute of the crossed leg.

exercises.position: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Cross your right ankle over your left knee. Pull your right toes back toward your shin.
exercises.then: Grab the back of your left thigh with both hands. Pull your left leg toward your chest.
exercises.tip: Push your right knee gently away from your body to feel a deeper stretch.

exercises.name: Figure Four Stretch - Left Hip
exercises.start: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.

steps.label: Cross Leg Over Knee
steps.cue: Lift your left foot off the floor and draw both legs toward your chest until you feel a stretch in your right hip.

steps.label: Draw In
steps.cue: Hold for the full duration. Use your hand to gently press your right knee outward to deepen the stretch. Switch sides when cued.
You should feel this in the outer hip and glute of the crossed leg.

exercises.position: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Cross your left ankle over your right knee. Pull your left toes back toward your shin.
exercises.then: Grab the back of your right thigh with both hands. Pull your right leg toward your chest.
exercises.tip: Push your left knee gently away from your body to feel a deeper stretch.

exercises.name: Windshield Wipers
exercises.start: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.

steps.label: Supine, Feet Flat
steps.cue: Keeping knees together, slowly lower both knees to the right as far as is comfortable. Keep both shoulders on the floor.

steps.label: Lower Knees Right
steps.cue: Sweep knees back through center and lower to the left. Move at a slow windshield-wiper pace. Continue alternating.

steps.label: Lower Knees Left
steps.cue: For an easier version, keep feet on the floor and let knees drop side to side from there.

exercises.position: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Reach your arms straight out to the sides. Keep your shoulders flat on the floor.
exercises.then: Let both knees drop slowly to the right side. Keep your feet apart.
exercises.tip: Lift your knees back to the middle, then let them drop slowly to the left side.

exercises.name: Reclined Spinal Twist - Right Side
exercises.start: Lie flat on your back with your legs straight.

steps.label: Supine Start
steps.cue: Guide that knee across your body with the opposite hand. Let it fall toward the floor.

steps.label: Cross & Hold
steps.cue: Turn your gaze in the opposite direction if comfortable. Hold for the full duration. Switch sides when cued.
Both shoulders stay on the floor. Let gravity do the work — do not force the knee down.

exercises.position: Lie flat on your back with your legs straight.
exercises.next: Pull your right knee to your chest. Reach your right arm straight out to the side.
exercises.then: Use your left hand to pull your right knee across your body toward the floor. Look to the right.
exercises.tip: Keep your right shoulder flat on the floor. Let gravity pull your knee down.

exercises.name: Reclined Spinal Twist - Left Side
exercises.start: Lie flat on your back with your legs straight.

steps.label: Supine Start
steps.cue: Guide that knee across your body with the opposite hand. Let it fall toward the floor.

steps.label: Cross & Hold
steps.cue: Turn your gaze in the opposite direction if comfortable. Hold for the full duration. Switch sides when cued.
Both shoulders stay on the floor. Let gravity do the work — do not force the knee down.

exercises.position: Lie flat on your back with your legs straight.
exercises.next: Pull your left knee to your chest. Reach your left arm straight out to the side.
exercises.then: Use your right hand to pull your left knee across your body toward the floor. Look to the left.
exercises.tip: Keep your left shoulder flat on the floor. Let gravity pull your knee down.

sections.label: Finish
sections.purpose: Signal to the nervous system that the work is done. These are decompression movements: not stretches to force, but positions to rest in. Take your time. There is no next thing.

exercises.name: Seated Forward Fold
exercises.start: Sit on the floor with your legs straight out in front of you.

steps.label: Seated Tall
steps.cue: Inhale to lengthen your spine. Hinge forward from your hips — not your waist — and walk your hands toward your feet.

steps.label: Fold Forward
steps.cue: Let your spine round naturally as you settle into the stretch. Head heavy. Hold for the full duration.
A long spine matters more than reaching your toes. Bend your knees slightly if your hamstrings are tight.

exercises.position: Sit on the floor with your legs straight out in front of you.
exercises.next: Sit up tall and take a deep breath.
exercises.then: Lean your chest forward toward your legs. Walk your hands down your legs toward your feet.
exercises.tip: Let your head hang heavy. Do not force yourself to reach your toes.

exercises.name: Chin Tucks
exercises.start: Sit or stand tall. Look straight ahead.

steps.label: Neutral Head
steps.cue: Draw your chin straight back — not down — as if making a double chin. The movement is purely horizontal.

steps.label: Retract
steps.cue: Hold for 2 seconds. Release fully. Repeat for the full duration.
You should feel a gentle stretch at the base of your skull. No tilting up or down.

exercises.position: Sit or stand tall. Look straight ahead.
exercises.next: Relax your shoulders down. Keep your eyes level.
exercises.then: Pull your chin straight back to make a double chin. Hold for 2 seconds.
exercises.tip: Keep your head level. Do not point your chin up or down.

exercises.name: Neck Half-Circles
exercises.start: Sit or stand tall. Look straight ahead.

steps.label: Neutral Start
steps.cue: Drop your right ear toward your right shoulder. Keep your left shoulder down. Hold 2 to 3 seconds. Return through center.

steps.label: Right Tilt
steps.cue: Drop your left ear toward your left shoulder. Smooth arc only — ear to ear through the front. Never through the back.

steps.label: Left Tilt
steps.cue: Do not roll the head backward. Keep the movement slow and controlled throughout.

exercises.position: Sit or stand tall. Look straight ahead.
exercises.next: Relax your shoulders down. Keep your chest lifted.
exercises.then: Drop your right ear toward your right shoulder. Hold for 2 seconds.
exercises.tip: Roll your chin slowly down to your chest, then lift your left ear toward your left shoulder.

restorative.label: Lymph & Flow
restorative.subtitle: Circulation, Mobility, Recovery
restorative.purpose: Gentle continuous movement for circulation, joint lubrication, and recovery.

sections.label: Full Reset
sections.purpose: Warm-up, circulation, and mobility. Each movement is 1 minute. This should feel easy, not tiring. Let the body move, not the mind.

exercises.name: Lymph Hops
exercises.start: Stand with your feet apart and knees bent.

steps.label: Soft Stand
steps.cue: Feet hip-width. Knees soft, never locked. Arms relaxed at sides. Jaw loose. Breathe naturally.

steps.label: Micro Bounce
steps.cue: Rise gently onto the balls of your feet and let your heels drop back down. Barely leave the ground. Think: soft vibration, not jumps.

steps.label: Rhythm
steps.cue: Find a relaxed 1–2 beat. Let arms dangle and bounce passively. Face and shoulders stay completely soft. This should feel effortless.

exercises.position: Stand with your feet apart and knees bent.
exercises.next: Let your arms hang loose at your sides. Relax your jaw. Breathe naturally.
exercises.then: Lift your heels off the floor, then lower them back down. Do not jump.
exercises.tip: Find a slow rhythm. Keep your face and shoulders soft.

exercises.name: Body Waves
exercises.start: Stand with your feet apart and knees slightly bent.

steps.label: Stand Loose
steps.cue: Feet hip-width, knees slightly bent. Arms at sides. Imagine your spine is a string of loose beads.

steps.label: Initiate Wave
steps.cue: Begin the wave at the top: let your head nod gently forward, then ripple the movement down through neck, chest, belly.

steps.label: Full Undulation
steps.cue: As the wave reaches the pelvis, let it curl forward then rise back up. Continuous, fluid figure-8. No forcing — gravity does the work.

exercises.position: Stand with your feet apart and knees slightly bent.
exercises.next: Let your arms hang loose at your sides.
exercises.then: Drop your head forward. Let the movement ripple down your neck, chest, and belly.
exercises.tip: When the wave reaches your hips, curl them forward and rise back up.

exercises.name: Arm Swings
exercises.start: Stand with your feet wide.

steps.label: Loose Stance
steps.cue: Feet slightly wider than hips. Knees soft. Let arms hang completely relaxed from the shoulders.

steps.label: Initiate Swing
steps.cue: Turn torso gently left — arms swing right passively, carried by momentum. Do not muscle the arms. They follow the body.

steps.label: Continuous Turn
steps.cue: Turn right — arms swing left. Find a pendulum rhythm. Arms may tap your sides lightly. Increase range gradually as you warm up.

exercises.position: Stand with your feet wide.
exercises.next: Keep your knees bent. Let your arms hang loose at your sides.
exercises.then: Turn your chest to the left. Let your arms swing to the right. Do not use your arm muscles.
exercises.tip: Turn your chest to the right. Let your arms swing to the left. Find a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Forward / Backward Arm Circles
exercises.start: Stand with your feet apart.

steps.label: Stand Tall
steps.cue: Feet hip-width. Arms at sides. Shoulders relaxed down. Begin with small circles and gradually increase the range.

steps.label: Forward Circles
steps.cue: Both arms circle forward simultaneously — 15 slow rotations. Feel the shoulder joint moving through its full range.

steps.label: Backward Circles
steps.cue: Reverse direction — 15 slow rotations backward. Backward circles open the chest and counteract forward shoulder posture.

exercises.position: Stand with your feet apart.
exercises.next: Keep your arms at your sides. Drop your shoulders down.
exercises.then: Move both arms in circles forward 15 times. Make the circles bigger as you go.
exercises.tip: Switch directions. Move both arms in circles backward 15 times.

exercises.name: Dead Arms
exercises.start: Stand and lean slightly forward from your hips.

steps.label: Slight Forward Lean
steps.cue: Hinge slightly at the hips. Let both arms hang completely dead — no muscle engagement. Jaw loose.

steps.label: Gentle Sway
steps.cue: Let the arms sway passively as you shift your weight side to side. Do not initiate any movement with the shoulder muscles. Gravity only.

steps.label: Small Circles
steps.cue: Let the arms trace small passive circles — clockwise, then counter-clockwise. This gently tractions the shoulder joints.

exercises.position: Stand and lean slightly forward from your hips.
exercises.next: Let both arms hang loose. Do not use your arm muscles. Relax your jaw.
exercises.then: Shift your weight from your right foot to your left foot. Let your arms swing side to side.
exercises.tip: Let your arms move in small circles. Keep your shoulders relaxed.

exercises.name: Bent Over Back Shakes
exercises.start: Stand and bend forward from your hips.

steps.label: Hinge Forward
steps.cue: Hinge at the hips, knees soft. Let the upper body hang forward — arms dangling, head heavy. Let gravity decompress the spine.

steps.label: Gentle Shake
steps.cue: Softly shake the upper body — small, effortless oscillations. Let the spine wobble freely. This releases the erectors and multifidus.

steps.label: Side Sway
steps.cue: Add a gentle side-to-side sway. Let the arms swing. Stay loose. This is pure release — nothing to engage.

exercises.position: Stand and bend forward from your hips.
exercises.next: Bend your knees slightly. Let your chest, head, and arms hang heavy toward the floor.
exercises.then: Shake your upper body gently side to side. Let your back wobble.
exercises.tip: Keep your arms loose. Do not use any muscles to hold yourself up.

exercises.name: Trunk Twists
exercises.start: Stand with your feet wide.

steps.label: Loose Stance
steps.cue: Feet shoulder-width, knees soft. Arms hang at sides. Spine tall but not stiff.

steps.label: Twist Right
steps.cue: Rotate torso to the right. Arms swing passively — left arm wraps across the front, right swings behind. Pivot on left ball of foot.

steps.label: Twist Left
steps.cue: Swing back through center and rotate left. Right arm wraps front, left swings behind. Find a loose, rhythmic pendulum pace.

exercises.position: Stand with your feet wide.
exercises.next: Let your arms hang loose at your sides. Keep your back straight but not stiff. Bend your knees slightly.
exercises.then: Turn your chest to the right. Let your left arm wrap across your front and your right arm swing behind you.
exercises.tip: Turn your chest to the left. Let your right arm wrap across your front. Find a steady rhythm.

exercises.name: Golf Swings
exercises.start: Stand with your feet wide and lean slightly forward from your hips.

steps.label: Address Position
steps.cue: Feet shoulder-width. Slight hip hinge. Arms hang loose. Imagine holding a club — but keep the grip soft.

steps.label: Backswing
steps.cue: Rotate through the hips and torso to the right, letting the arms follow passively. Left heel can lift slightly. No forcing.

steps.label: Through Swing
steps.cue: Swing through to the left in one fluid motion. Arms follow the torso — do not lead with the arms. Alternate sides for 1 minute.

exercises.position: Stand with your feet wide and lean slightly forward from your hips.
exercises.next: Let your arms hang loose. Pretend you are holding a golf club loosely.
exercises.then: Turn your hips and chest to the right. Let your arms follow. You can lift your left heel.
exercises.tip: Swing your arms to the left in one smooth motion. Do not force the movement.

exercises.name: Twist the Waist
exercises.start: Stand with your feet apart and hands on your hips.
exercises.sub: Isolated hip rotation, upper body still

steps.label: Stand Tall
steps.cue: Feet hip-width. Hands on hips. Eyes forward. Upper body completely still throughout — only the hips move.

steps.label: Hip Rotation
steps.cue: Rotate the pelvis to the right, then sweep left in a continuous figure-8 or side-to-side motion. Upper body stays square.

steps.label: Find Rhythm
steps.cue: Increase the range gradually. Feel the lumbar spine rotating freely. This is the movement that desk work robs you of most.

exercises.position: Stand with your feet apart and hands on your hips.
exercises.next: Look straight ahead. Keep your chest and shoulders completely still.
exercises.then: Move your hips to the right, then sweep them to the left in a circle.
exercises.tip: Make the circles bigger as you go. Move only your hips.

exercises.name: March Taps
exercises.start: Stand tall with your shoulders relaxed down.

steps.label: Upright Stance
steps.cue: Stand tall. Shoulders relaxed. This is a gentle march — not a high-knee drill. Pace is easy and rhythmic.

steps.label: Lift & Tap
steps.cue: Lift right knee to comfortable height, tap with left hand. Lower. Lift left knee, tap with right hand. Alternate continuously.

steps.label: Add Arm Swing
steps.cue: Let the opposite arm swing naturally with each step — just like walking. This cross-body pattern activates lymphatic flow bilaterally.

exercises.position: Stand tall with your shoulders relaxed down.
exercises.next: Keep a steady, easy pace. Do not lift your knees too high.
exercises.then: Lift your right knee and tap it with your left hand. Lower it. Lift your left knee and tap it with your right hand.
exercises.tip: Let your other arm swing naturally, like you are walking.

exercises.name: Wide Arm Step Backs
exercises.start: Stand with your feet together and arms at your sides.
exercises.sub: Lateral step with full arm opening

steps.label: Neutral Start
steps.cue: Feet together, arms at sides. Soft knees. This movement is slow and deliberate — not aerobic.

steps.label: Step & Open
steps.cue: Step right foot back and wide as both arms sweep out to the sides and overhead — like opening a large book. Inhale.

steps.label: Return & Close
steps.cue: Step feet back together, arms return to sides. Exhale. Alternate sides. The arm sweep opens the chest and axillary lymph nodes.

exercises.position: Stand with your feet together and arms at your sides.
exercises.next: Bend your knees slightly. Move slowly.
exercises.then: Step your right foot backward and wide to the side. Reach both arms out to the sides and up to the ceiling.
exercises.tip: Step your feet back together and lower your arms. Switch to the left foot.

exercises.name: Ballet Squats
exercises.start: Stand with your feet wide and toes pointing out to the sides.
exercises.sub: Wide stance pliés, toes turned out

steps.label: Wide Stance
steps.cue: Feet wider than hips, toes turned out to roughly 10 and 2 o'clock. Arms out at sides or hands at heart center.

steps.label: Plié Down
steps.cue: Bend knees, tracking them over toes. Lower slowly — 3 seconds down. Keep torso upright, tailbone dropping straight down.

steps.label: Rise Up
steps.cue: Press through the full foot to return to standing — 2 seconds up. Gently squeeze inner thighs at the top. Do not lock knees.

exercises.position: Stand with your feet wide and toes pointing out to the sides.
exercises.next: Bring your hands to your chest.
exercises.then: Bend your knees. Lower your hips straight down toward the floor for 3 seconds.
exercises.tip: Press your feet into the floor to stand back up for 2 seconds. Keep your knees slightly bent at the top.

exercises.name: Tip Toe Arm Swings
exercises.start: Stand with your feet apart.
exercises.sub: Rising onto toes with overhead reach

steps.label: Flat Foot
steps.cue: Feet hip-width. Arms relaxed at sides. Soft knees. This is the low point of the movement.

steps.label: Rise & Reach
steps.cue: Rise onto tiptoes as arms sweep upward and forward to shoulder height or overhead. Inhale on the rise.

steps.label: Lower & Swing
steps.cue: Lower heels as arms swing back down and behind. Exhale. The arm swing is passive — gravity-assisted. Continuous and flowing.

exercises.position: Stand with your feet apart.
exercises.next: Let your arms hang loose at your sides. Bend your knees slightly.
exercises.then: Lift your heels off the floor. Swing your arms forward and up to the ceiling.
exercises.tip: Lower your heels to the floor. Let your arms swing down and behind you.

exercises.name: Doorway Pec Stretch
exercises.start: Stand in a doorway.

steps.label: Doorway Position
steps.cue: Stand in a doorway. Place both forearms on the frame at 90° — elbows at shoulder height, upper arms parallel to floor.

steps.label: Step Through
steps.cue: Step one foot forward through the doorway. Let your body lean gently into the opening. Feel the chest and front shoulders open.

steps.label: Hold & Breathe
steps.cue: Hold without forcing. Take 3 slow, deep breaths — each exhale, let the chest open a little more. Do not shrug shoulders.

exercises.position: Stand in a doorway.
exercises.next: Place both forearms flat on the door frame. Keep your elbows bent at shoulder height.
exercises.then: Step one foot forward through the doorway. Lean your chest gently forward.
exercises.tip: Take 3 slow breaths. Let your chest drop forward a little more each time you breathe out.

exercises.name: Wall Pushups
exercises.start: Stand facing a wall, one arm's length away.

steps.label: Hands on Wall
steps.cue: Stand an arm's length from the wall. Place hands at shoulder height, slightly wider than shoulder-width. Body in a straight line.

steps.label: Lower In
steps.cue: Bend elbows and lean chest toward the wall. Keep the body rigid — no sagging hips. Elbows track at roughly 45° from the torso.

steps.label: Press Out
steps.cue: Push back to start. Exhale on the push. Move slowly and with control. This is not about speed — it's about reactivating the chest and shoulders.

exercises.position: Stand facing a wall, one arm's length away.
exercises.next: Place your hands flat on the wall at shoulder height. Keep your body straight.
exercises.then: Bend your elbows. Lean your chest toward the wall. Keep your hips in line with your shoulders.
exercises.tip: Push the wall away to straighten your arms. Move slowly.

morning.label: Sunrise Stretch
morning.subtitle: Wake, Spine, Energy
morning.purpose: Seven movements to wake the spine before you look at your phone.

sections.label: Morning Refresh
sections.purpose: Wake the spine and nervous system gently. These seven movements take under 8 minutes and set the tone for how your body moves all day. Do them before you look at your phone.

exercises.name: Supine Knee Hugs
exercises.start: Lie on your back with your legs straight.

steps.label: Flat on Back
steps.cue: Lie on your back. Let the body be completely heavy. Take two slow breaths before moving.

steps.label: Hug Both Knees
steps.cue: Draw both knees to chest. Wrap arms around shins. Rock gently side to side 5–8 times. Release lower back.
Keep the movement small and gentle. This is decompression, not a stretch.

exercises.position: Lie on your back with your legs straight.
exercises.next: Relax your body completely. Take two slow breaths.
exercises.then: Pull both knees to your chest. Wrap your arms around your shins.
exercises.tip: Rock gently from left to right. Keep the movement small.

exercises.name: Supine Twist - Right Side
exercises.start: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.

steps.label: Knees to Chest
steps.cue: Lie on back, knees bent and together. Arms out in a T. Shoulders heavy.

steps.label: Drop Right
steps.cue: Let both knees fall to the right. Turn gaze left if comfortable. Breathe and let gravity do the work for 30 seconds.

steps.label: Drop Left
steps.cue: Return knees through center, drop left. Hold another 30 seconds. No forcing — just releasing.

exercises.position: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Reach your arms straight out to the sides. Keep your shoulders flat on the floor.
exercises.then: Let both knees drop to the right side. Turn your head to the left. Hold for 30 seconds.
exercises.tip: Bring your knees back to the middle, then let them drop to the left side. Hold for 30 seconds.

exercises.name: Supine Twist - Left Side
exercises.start: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.

steps.label: Knees to Chest
steps.cue: Lie on back, knees bent and together. Arms out in a T. Shoulders heavy.

steps.label: Drop Right
steps.cue: Let both knees fall to the right. Turn gaze left if comfortable. Breathe and let gravity do the work for 30 seconds.

steps.label: Drop Left
steps.cue: Return knees through center, drop left. Hold another 30 seconds. No forcing — just releasing.

exercises.position: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Reach your arms straight out to the sides. Keep your shoulders flat on the floor.
exercises.then: Let both knees drop to the right side. Turn your head to the left. Hold for 30 seconds.
exercises.tip: Bring your knees back to the middle, then let them drop to the left side. Hold for 30 seconds.

exercises.name: Cat–Cow
exercises.start: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.

steps.label: Neutral Tabletop
steps.cue: Hands under shoulders, knees under hips. Spine flat. Take one breath here before moving.

steps.label: Cat
steps.cue: Round spine to ceiling, tuck pelvis and chin. Full exhale.

steps.label: Cow
steps.cue: Let belly drop, lift gaze and tailbone gently. Full inhale. 8–10 slow cycles.

exercises.position: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.
exercises.next: Place your hands under your shoulders and your knees under your hips. Keep your back flat.
exercises.then: Push your back up toward the ceiling. Tuck your chin to your chest.
exercises.tip: Let your belly drop toward the floor. Lift your head and tailbone up. Move slowly.

exercises.name: Standing Hip Circles
exercises.start: Stand with your feet apart and hands on your hips.

steps.label: Stand Tall
steps.cue: Feet hip-width. Hands on hips. Soft knees. Eyes forward or closed.

steps.label: Circle Right
steps.cue: Make slow, large circles with the pelvis — forward, right, back, left. 6 circles clockwise.

steps.label: Circle Left
steps.cue: Reverse direction. 6 circles counter-clockwise. Let the hips move freely without rushing.

exercises.position: Stand with your feet apart and hands on your hips.
exercises.next: Bend your knees slightly. Look straight ahead.
exercises.then: Move your hips in slow, large circles to the right 6 times.
exercises.tip: Move your hips in circles to the left 6 times. Move slowly.

exercises.name: Shoulder Rolls
exercises.start: Stand or sit tall.

steps.label: Arms at Sides
steps.cue: Stand or sit tall. Arms completely relaxed. Jaw soft.

steps.label: Roll Forward
steps.cue: Shrug shoulders up toward ears, then roll them forward and down. 6 slow rolls.

steps.label: Roll Back
steps.cue: Reverse — shrug up, roll back and down. 6 rolls. Feel the shoulder blades move and the chest open.

exercises.position: Stand or sit tall.
exercises.next: Let your arms hang loose at your sides. Relax your jaw. Keep your arms completely still.
exercises.then: Lift your shoulders up to your ears, then roll them forward and down. Do this 6 times.
exercises.tip: Lift your shoulders up, then roll them backward and down. Do this 6 times.

exercises.name: Sun Breath
exercises.start: Stand with your feet apart and arms at your sides.

steps.label: Stand Grounded
steps.cue: Feet hip-width. Arms at sides. Close the eyes. Feel the weight of the body through the soles of the feet.

steps.label: Inhale & Rise
steps.cue: Breathe in slowly for 4 counts as arms sweep out to the sides and overhead. Palms meet or stay wide.

steps.label: Exhale & Lower
steps.cue: Breathe out for 6 counts as arms float back down. Feel the chest and ribcage expand on the inhale. 6 full cycles.

exercises.position: Stand with your feet apart and arms at your sides.
exercises.next: Press your feet firmly into the floor.
exercises.then: Breathe in slowly. Reach your arms out to the sides and up to the ceiling.
exercises.tip: Breathe out slowly and lower your arms back to your sides. Do this 6 times.

prime.label: Night Ease
prime.subtitle: Decompress, Calm, Sleep
prime.purpose: Lower cortisol, decompress the spine, prepare for sleep.

sections.label: Better Sleep Stretch
sections.purpose: A deliberate transition from doing to being. These movements lower cortisol, decompress the spine after a day of load, and prepare the nervous system for sleep. Move slowly. There is nothing left to do.

exercises.name: Legs Up the Wall
exercises.start: Sit on the floor close to a wall.

steps.label: Setup
steps.cue: Sit sideways against the wall, then swing legs up. Hips as close to the wall as comfortable. Arms out at sides, palms up.

steps.label: Hold & Breathe
steps.cue: Let gravity drain the legs. Close the eyes. Breathe naturally. This posture reverses venous pooling and calms the nervous system.
Place a folded blanket under your hips if your lower back is tight. Stay as long as feels good.

exercises.position: Sit on the floor close to a wall.
exercises.next: Lie on your back and swing your legs up the wall. Move your hips close to the wall.
exercises.then: Reach your arms out to the sides with your palms facing up. Close your eyes.
exercises.tip: Place a folded blanket under your hips if your lower back feels tight.

exercises.name: Reclined Butterfly
exercises.start: Lie on your back with your feet flat on the floor.

steps.label: Soles Together
steps.cue: Lie on back. Bring the soles of your feet together and let knees fall wide. Place hands on belly or out at sides.

steps.label: Gravity Hold
steps.cue: Let the inner thighs and groin soften completely. Do not push. Hold 60–90s.
Support the outer thighs with pillows if the stretch feels too intense.

exercises.position: Lie on your back with your feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Bring the bottoms of your feet together. Let your knees drop open to the sides.
exercises.then: Place your hands on your belly or out to the sides. Hold for 60 to 90 seconds.
exercises.tip: Place pillows under your knees if the stretch is too intense.

exercises.name: Knees to Chest Rock
exercises.start: Lie flat on your back.

steps.label: Hug Knees
steps.cue: Lie on back. Hug both knees to chest. Interlock fingers over shins.

steps.label: Rock Side to Side
steps.cue: Rock gently left and right. Let the lower back release against the floor. 10–15 slow rocks.
Move slowly. This is a massage for your lower back — let the floor do the work.

exercises.position: Lie flat on your back.
exercises.next: Pull both knees to your chest. Clasp your hands over your shins.
exercises.then: Rock slowly from left to right. Let your lower back press into the floor.
exercises.tip: Move slowly. Let the floor massage your back.

exercises.name: Supine Twist - Right Side
exercises.start: Lie on your back with your legs straight.

steps.label: Setup
steps.cue: Lie on back. Draw right knee to chest. Arms in a T, shoulders grounded.

steps.label: Cross Right
steps.cue: Guide right knee across to the left with left hand. Turn gaze right. Hold for 45–60s.

steps.label: Cross Left
steps.cue: Return through center. Draw left knee across right. Gaze left. Hold another 45–60s.

exercises.position: Lie on your back with your legs straight.
exercises.next: Pull your right knee to your chest. Reach your arms out to the sides.
exercises.then: Use your left hand to pull your right knee across your body to the left side. Turn your head to the right. Hold.
exercises.tip: Bring your right knee back to the middle. Pull your left knee across to the right side. Hold.

exercises.name: Supine Twist - Left Side
exercises.start: Lie on your back with your legs straight.

steps.label: Setup
steps.cue: Lie on back. Draw right knee to chest. Arms in a T, shoulders grounded.

steps.label: Cross Right
steps.cue: Guide right knee across to the left with left hand. Turn gaze right. Hold for 45–60s.

steps.label: Cross Left
steps.cue: Return through center. Draw left knee across right. Gaze left. Hold another 45–60s.

exercises.position: Lie on your back with your legs straight.
exercises.next: Pull your right knee to your chest. Reach your arms out to the sides.
exercises.then: Use your left hand to pull your right knee across your body to the left side. Turn your head to the right. Hold.
exercises.tip: Bring your right knee back to the middle. Pull your left knee across to the right side. Hold.

exercises.name: 4-7-8 Breath
exercises.start: Sit or lie down in a comfortable position.

steps.label: Comfortable Position
steps.cue: Lie flat or sit upright. Eyes closed. Place one hand on the chest, one on the belly.

steps.label: Inhale 4
steps.cue: Breathe in through the nose for 4 slow counts. Feel the belly rise before the chest.

steps.label: Hold 7, Exhale 8
steps.cue: Hold for 7 counts. Exhale completely through the mouth for 8 counts. Repeat 4–6 cycles.

exercises.position: Sit or lie down in a comfortable position.
exercises.next: Close your eyes. Place one hand on your chest and one hand on your belly.
exercises.then: Breathe in through your nose for 4 seconds. Feel your belly lift before your chest.
exercises.tip: Hold your breath for 7 seconds. Breathe out through your mouth for 8 seconds. Repeat 4 times.

desk.label: Desk Undo
desk.subtitle: Posture, Sitting, Refresh
desk.purpose: Six movements to undo an hour of sitting. Stand up. Do it now.

sections.label: The Desk Break
sections.purpose: Six movements to undo an hour of sitting. Stand up and do it now — don't wait for a better time.

exercises.name: Standing Chest Opener
exercises.start: Stand tall away from your chair.

steps.label: Stand Tall
steps.cue: Stand away from your chair. Feet hip-width. Interlace fingers behind your back.

steps.label: Open & Lift
steps.cue: Straighten arms, draw shoulder blades together, lift hands slightly. Open chest toward ceiling. Hold 20–30s.
Do not push the lift aggressively. Focus on drawing the shoulder blades together, not lifting the arms high.

exercises.position: Stand tall away from your chair.
exercises.next: Clasp your hands together behind your back. Keep your feet apart.
exercises.then: Straighten your arms. Squeeze your shoulder blades together and lift your hands slightly. Hold.
exercises.tip: Focus on squeezing your shoulder blades together, not lifting your arms high.

exercises.name: Chin Tucks
exercises.start: Sit or stand tall. Look straight ahead.

steps.label: Neutral Head
steps.cue: Sit or stand tall. Eyes level. Shoulders relaxed.

steps.label: Retract
steps.cue: Draw chin straight back — not down. Create a slight double chin. Hold 2s. 10 reps.
You should feel a gentle stretch at the base of your skull. No tilting up or down.

exercises.position: Sit or stand tall. Look straight ahead.
exercises.next: Relax your shoulders down. Keep your eyes level.
exercises.then: Pull your chin straight back to make a double chin. Hold for 2 seconds. Do 10 times.
exercises.tip: Do not look up or down. Keep your head level.

exercises.name: Thoracic Rotations - Right Side
exercises.start: Sit at the front edge of a chair with your feet flat on the floor.

steps.label: Sit Tall
steps.cue: Sit at edge of chair. Feet flat. Interlace hands behind head. Elbows wide. Spine long.

steps.label: Rotate Right
steps.cue: Rotate upper body to the right — elbows leading. Keep hips square. Hold 2s at end range.

steps.label: Rotate Left
steps.cue: Return through center, rotate left. Equal range each side. The rotation comes from the mid-back, not the neck.

exercises.position: Sit at the front edge of a chair with your feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Clasp your hands behind your head. Point your elbows out to the sides.
exercises.then: Turn your chest and elbows to the right. Keep your hips firmly on the chair.
exercises.tip: Turn your chest back to the middle, then turn to the left. Keep your neck straight.

exercises.name: Thoracic Rotations - Left Side
exercises.start: Sit at the front edge of a chair with your feet flat on the floor.

steps.label: Sit Tall
steps.cue: Sit at edge of chair. Feet flat. Interlace hands behind head. Elbows wide. Spine long.

steps.label: Rotate Right
steps.cue: Rotate upper body to the right — elbows leading. Keep hips square. Hold 2s at end range.

steps.label: Rotate Left
steps.cue: Return through center, rotate left. Equal range each side. The rotation comes from the mid-back, not the neck.

exercises.position: Sit at the front edge of a chair with your feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Clasp your hands behind your head. Point your elbows out to the sides.
exercises.then: Turn your chest and elbows to the right. Keep your hips firmly on the chair.
exercises.tip: Turn your chest back to the middle, then turn to the left. Keep your neck straight.

exercises.name: Standing Hip Flexor Stretch - Right Hip
exercises.start: Kneel on the floor with your right knee down.

steps.label: Half Kneel
steps.cue: Kneel on right knee, left foot forward. Hips square. Hands on left thigh for support.

steps.label: Shift Forward
steps.cue: Gently shift weight forward until you feel a stretch in the right hip flexor. Keep torso upright. Hold 30s each side.
Keep your hips square throughout. Do not let the front knee cave inward.

exercises.position: Kneel on the floor with your right knee down.
exercises.next: Place your left foot flat on the floor in front of you. Point both hip bones forward. Place your hands on your left thigh.
exercises.then: Lean your hips slowly forward until you feel a pull in your right thigh. Keep your chest up. Hold.
exercises.tip: Do not let your front knee push past your toes. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Standing Hip Flexor Stretch - Left Hip
exercises.start: Kneel on the floor with your right knee down.

steps.label: Half Kneel
steps.cue: Kneel on right knee, left foot forward. Hips square. Hands on left thigh for support.

steps.label: Shift Forward
steps.cue: Gently shift weight forward until you feel a stretch in the right hip flexor. Keep torso upright. Hold 30s each side.
Keep your hips square throughout. Do not let the front knee cave inward.

exercises.position: Kneel on the floor with your right knee down.
exercises.next: Place your left foot flat on the floor in front of you. Point both hip bones forward. Place your hands on your left thigh.
exercises.then: Lean your hips slowly forward until you feel a pull in your right thigh. Keep your chest up. Hold.
exercises.tip: Do not let your front knee push past your toes. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Wrist & Forearm Release
exercises.start: Sit or stand. Reach one arm straight out in front of you.

steps.label: Extend Arm
steps.cue: Extend one arm forward, palm facing down. Use other hand to gently pull fingers back toward you.

steps.label: Flex Stretch
steps.cue: Hold 15–20s. Feel the forearm extensors lengthen. Reverse — palm up, pull fingers down. Hold 15–20s. Switch arms.
Go gently — these muscles are often tight from keyboard and mouse use. Never force the stretch.

exercises.position: Sit or stand. Reach one arm straight out in front of you.
exercises.next: Point your fingers up toward the ceiling.
exercises.then: Use your other hand to gently pull your fingers back toward your body. Hold.
exercises.tip: Point your fingers down toward the floor and gently pull them toward your body. Switch arms.

exercises.name: Neck Side Tilts - Right Neck
exercises.start: Sit or stand tall. Look straight ahead.

steps.label: Sit Tall
steps.cue: Sit at edge of chair. Spine long. Shoulders down and relaxed.

steps.label: Drop Right
steps.cue: Let right ear fall toward right shoulder. Gravity only — no forcing. Left shoulder stays grounded. 20s.

steps.label: Drop Left
steps.cue: Return through center. Drop left ear. 20s. Return slowly.

exercises.position: Sit or stand tall. Look straight ahead.
exercises.next: Relax your shoulders down away from your ears.
exercises.then: Drop your right ear toward your right shoulder. Keep your left shoulder down. Hold.
exercises.tip: Bring your head back to the middle. Drop your left ear toward your left shoulder. Hold.

exercises.name: Neck Side Tilts - Left Neck
exercises.start: Sit or stand tall. Look straight ahead.

steps.label: Sit Tall
steps.cue: Sit at edge of chair. Spine long. Shoulders down and relaxed.

steps.label: Drop Right
steps.cue: Let right ear fall toward right shoulder. Gravity only — no forcing. Left shoulder stays grounded. 20s.

steps.label: Drop Left
steps.cue: Return through center. Drop left ear. 20s. Return slowly.

exercises.position: Sit or stand tall. Look straight ahead.
exercises.next: Relax your shoulders down away from your ears.
exercises.then: Drop your right ear toward your right shoulder. Keep your left shoulder down. Hold.
exercises.tip: Bring your head back to the middle. Drop your left ear toward your left shoulder. Hold.

screen.label: Tech Neck Release
screen.subtitle: Neck, Shoulder, Screen
screen.purpose: Undo the forward pull of screen time. Seven targeted releases for the neck and shoulder girdle.

sections.label: Digital Detox
sections.purpose: The neck and upper traps absorb everything — hours of screen time, stress, poor posture, shallow breathing. This session undoes it. Move slowly and without urgency. This area responds to patience, not force.

exercises.name: Chin Tucks
exercises.start: Sit tall with your eyes looking straight ahead.

steps.label: Sit or Stand Tall
steps.cue: Eyes level with horizon. Shoulders relaxed down. Jaw soft. This is the single most important correction for forward head posture.

steps.label: Retract
steps.cue: Draw chin straight back — not down. Create a slight double chin. Hold 2s. Feel a stretch at the base of the skull. Release slowly. 10 reps.
You should feel a gentle stretch at the base of the skull, not the front of the throat.

exercises.position: Sit tall with your eyes looking straight ahead.
exercises.next: Relax your shoulders down. Relax your jaw.
exercises.then: Pull your chin straight back to make a double chin. Hold for 2 seconds. Slowly release. Do 10 times.
exercises.tip: Keep your head level. Do not point your chin up or down.

exercises.name: Neck Side Tilt - Right Neck
exercises.start: Sit tall with your shoulders relaxed.

steps.label: Sit Tall
steps.cue: Sit or stand. Spine long. Shoulders relaxed down away from ears. Eyes forward.

steps.label: Right Ear Down
steps.cue: Let the right ear fall toward the right shoulder. Do not force — use gravity only. Hold 30s. Left shoulder stays down.

steps.label: Left Ear Down
steps.cue: Return through center, drop left ear. Hold 30s. For deeper release, rest hand lightly on top of head — do not pull.

exercises.position: Sit tall with your shoulders relaxed.
exercises.next: Keep your back straight and look straight ahead.
exercises.then: Drop your right ear toward your right shoulder. Keep your left shoulder down. Hold for 30 seconds.
exercises.tip: Bring your head back to the middle. Drop your left ear toward your left shoulder. Hold for 30 seconds.

exercises.name: Neck Side Tilt - Left Neck
exercises.start: Sit tall with your shoulders relaxed.

steps.label: Sit Tall
steps.cue: Sit or stand. Spine long. Shoulders relaxed down away from ears. Eyes forward.

steps.label: Right Ear Down
steps.cue: Let the right ear fall toward the right shoulder. Do not force — use gravity only. Hold 30s. Left shoulder stays down.

steps.label: Left Ear Down
steps.cue: Return through center, drop left ear. Hold 30s. For deeper release, rest hand lightly on top of head — do not pull.

exercises.position: Sit tall with your shoulders relaxed.
exercises.next: Keep your back straight and look straight ahead.
exercises.then: Drop your right ear toward your right shoulder. Keep your left shoulder down. Hold for 30 seconds.
exercises.tip: Bring your head back to the middle. Drop your left ear toward your left shoulder. Hold for 30 seconds.

exercises.name: Levator Scapulae Stretch - Right Side
exercises.start: Sit tall in a chair.

steps.label: Turn & Tilt
steps.cue: Turn head 45° to the right and tilt chin toward the right armpit. Feel the stretch from the base of the skull toward the left shoulder blade.

steps.label: Add Weight
steps.cue: Rest the right hand gently on the back of the head — do not pull. Hold 30s. Switch sides. This targets the levator scapulae, the most screen-tension muscle.
Never pull the head with force. The weight of the hand is enough. Breathe into the stretch.

exercises.position: Sit tall in a chair.
exercises.next: Turn your head to the right and drop your chin toward your right armpit.
exercises.then: Place your right hand gently on the back of your head. Do not pull. Hold for 30 seconds.
exercises.tip: Let the weight of your hand do the work. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Levator Scapulae Stretch - Left Side
exercises.start: Sit tall in a chair.

steps.label: Turn & Tilt
steps.cue: Turn head 45° to the right and tilt chin toward the right armpit. Feel the stretch from the base of the skull toward the left shoulder blade.

steps.label: Add Weight
steps.cue: Rest the right hand gently on the back of the head — do not pull. Hold 30s. Switch sides. This targets the levator scapulae, the most screen-tension muscle.
Never pull the head with force. The weight of the hand is enough. Breathe into the stretch.

exercises.position: Sit tall in a chair.
exercises.next: Turn your head to the right and drop your chin toward your right armpit.
exercises.then: Place your right hand gently on the back of your head. Do not pull. Hold for 30 seconds.
exercises.tip: Let the weight of your hand do the work. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Upper Trap Release
exercises.start: Sit on a chair. Place your right hand under your right hip.

steps.label: Anchor Shoulder
steps.cue: Sit on right hand to anchor the right shoulder down. Sit tall. This prevents the shoulder from shrugging up and reduces the stretch.

steps.label: Lean Left
steps.cue: With right shoulder anchored, slowly tilt the head to the left and slightly forward. Feel the right upper trap lengthen. Hold 30s. Switch.
The anchoring hand is essential — without it the shoulder shrugs up and the stretch is lost.

exercises.position: Sit on a chair. Place your right hand under your right hip.
exercises.next: Sit up tall. Keep your right hand trapped under your hip to hold your shoulder down.
exercises.then: Drop your left ear toward your left shoulder and lean your head slightly forward. Hold for 30 seconds.
exercises.tip: Keep your right shoulder pressed down. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Thread the Needle - Right Side
exercises.start: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.

steps.label: Tabletop
steps.cue: Hands under shoulders, knees under hips. Spine neutral. Take a breath before moving.

steps.label: Thread Right
steps.cue: Slide right arm along the floor under the left arm. Right shoulder and ear rest on floor. Hold 30s. Feel the mid-back open.

steps.label: Thread Left
steps.cue: Return to tabletop. Left arm threads under right. Hold 30s each side. This releases the thoracic spine that screen time locks up.

exercises.position: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.
exercises.next: Place your hands under your shoulders and your knees under your hips. Keep your back flat.
exercises.then: Slide your right arm under your left arm. Rest your right shoulder and the right side of your head on the floor. Hold for 30 seconds.
exercises.tip: Push back up to your hands and knees. Slide your left arm under your right arm. Hold for 30 seconds.

exercises.name: Thread the Needle - Left Side
exercises.start: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.

steps.label: Tabletop
steps.cue: Hands under shoulders, knees under hips. Spine neutral. Take a breath before moving.

steps.label: Thread Right
steps.cue: Slide right arm along the floor under the left arm. Right shoulder and ear rest on floor. Hold 30s. Feel the mid-back open.

steps.label: Thread Left
steps.cue: Return to tabletop. Left arm threads under right. Hold 30s each side. This releases the thoracic spine that screen time locks up.

exercises.position: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.
exercises.next: Place your hands under your shoulders and your knees under your hips. Keep your back flat.
exercises.then: Slide your right arm under your left arm. Rest your right shoulder and the right side of your head on the floor. Hold for 30 seconds.
exercises.tip: Push back up to your hands and knees. Slide your left arm under your right arm. Hold for 30 seconds.

exercises.name: Eagle Arms
exercises.start: Sit or stand tall with your arms at your sides.

steps.label: Cross Arms
steps.cue: Sit or stand tall. Cross right arm over left at the elbows. Wrap forearms and try to bring palms together.

steps.label: Lift & Hold
steps.cue: Lift elbows to shoulder height. Feel the space between the shoulder blades open. Hold 30s. Switch arm cross. Targets the rhomboids.
If palms do not meet, press the backs of the hands together instead. The lift is more important than the wrap.

exercises.position: Sit or stand tall with your arms at your sides.
exercises.next: Cross your right arm over your left arm at the elbows.
exercises.then: Bend your elbows and twist your lower arms together so your hands meet. Lift your elbows up to shoulder height. Hold for 30 seconds.
exercises.tip: If your hands cannot meet, press the backs of your hands together instead. Switch the arm cross.

exercises.name: Chest Opener with Clasp
exercises.start: Stand tall. Clasp your hands together behind your back.

steps.label: Clasp Behind
steps.cue: Stand tall. Interlace fingers behind the back. Straighten arms. Draw shoulder blades together gently.

steps.label: Lift & Open
steps.cue: Lift clasped hands slightly away from the body. Open the chest. Chin slightly up. Hold 30s. This directly counters the forward curl of screen posture.
Do not compress the lower back by arching. Keep your core gently engaged throughout.

exercises.position: Stand tall. Clasp your hands together behind your back.
exercises.next: Straighten your arms. Squeeze your shoulder blades together.
exercises.then: Lift your hands slowly away from your hips. Lift your chest and look slightly up. Hold for 30 seconds.
exercises.tip: Keep your stomach muscles tight. Do not arch your lower back.

hip.label: Hip Freedom
hip.subtitle: Open, Mobilize, Rotators
hip.purpose: Open the hips, free the lower back, restore the range that sitting steals.

sections.label: Happy Hips
sections.purpose: The hips are the body's centre of power — and the first thing to tighten from sitting. Restricted hips pull on the lower back, destabilise the knees, and shorten the stride. These holds release the hip flexors, rotators, and adductors. Stay in each position. Let gravity and breath do the work.

exercises.name: 90/90 Hip Stretch - Right Hip
exercises.start: Sit on the floor. Bend both knees so your legs form 90-degree angles.

steps.label: Sit to Floor
steps.cue: Sit on the floor. Right leg bent in front at 90°, knee and ankle on the ground. Left leg bent behind at 90°. Both shins parallel.

steps.label: Sit Tall Over Front Leg
steps.cue: Sit upright over the front (right) shin. Feel the external rotation of the right hip. Press the right knee gently toward the floor. Hold 60s.

steps.label: Hinge Forward
steps.cue: Hinge at the hips and walk hands forward over the front shin. Keep spine long — do not round. Hold 30s additional. Switch sides.

exercises.position: Sit on the floor. Bend both knees so your legs form 90-degree angles.
exercises.next: Place your right leg flat on the floor in front of you. Place your left leg flat on the floor behind you.
exercises.then: Sit tall over your right leg. Press your right knee down into the floor. Hold for 60 seconds.
exercises.tip: Lean your chest forward over your right leg. Keep your back straight. Switch sides.

exercises.name: 90/90 Hip Stretch - Left Hip
exercises.start: Sit on the floor. Bend both knees so your legs form 90-degree angles.

steps.label: Sit to Floor
steps.cue: Sit on the floor. Right leg bent in front at 90°, knee and ankle on the ground. Left leg bent behind at 90°. Both shins parallel.

steps.label: Sit Tall Over Front Leg
steps.cue: Sit upright over the front (right) shin. Feel the external rotation of the right hip. Press the right knee gently toward the floor. Hold 60s.

steps.label: Hinge Forward
steps.cue: Hinge at the hips and walk hands forward over the front shin. Keep spine long — do not round. Hold 30s additional. Switch sides.

exercises.position: Sit on the floor. Bend both knees so your legs form 90-degree angles.
exercises.next: Place your right leg flat on the floor in front of you. Place your left leg flat on the floor behind you.
exercises.then: Sit tall over your right leg. Press your right knee down into the floor. Hold for 60 seconds.
exercises.tip: Lean your chest forward over your right leg. Keep your back straight. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Kneeling Hip Flexor Stretch - Right Hip
exercises.start: Kneel on the floor with your right knee down. Place your left foot flat in front of you.

steps.label: Half Kneel
steps.cue: Kneel on right knee, left foot forward. Hips square. Stack left knee over left ankle. Place hands on left thigh.

steps.label: Shift & Tuck
steps.cue: Shift hips forward until you feel a deep stretch in the right hip flexor and front of the right thigh. Tuck the pelvis slightly under — do not arch the lower back.

steps.label: Arm Reach
steps.cue: Reach right arm overhead and lean slightly left. This lengthens the hip flexor through the full chain to the ribcage. Hold 60s. Switch sides.

exercises.position: Kneel on the floor with your right knee down. Place your left foot flat in front of you.
exercises.next: Place your hands on your left thigh. Point both hip bones straight forward.
exercises.then: Push your hips forward until you feel a pull in your right thigh. Do not arch your lower back.
exercises.tip: Reach your right arm up to the ceiling and lean slightly to the left. Hold for 60 seconds. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Kneeling Hip Flexor Stretch - Left Hip
exercises.start: Kneel on the floor with your right knee down. Place your left foot flat in front of you.

steps.label: Half Kneel
steps.cue: Kneel on right knee, left foot forward. Hips square. Stack left knee over left ankle. Place hands on left thigh.

steps.label: Shift & Tuck
steps.cue: Shift hips forward until you feel a deep stretch in the right hip flexor and front of the right thigh. Tuck the pelvis slightly under — do not arch the lower back.

steps.label: Arm Reach
steps.cue: Reach right arm overhead and lean slightly left. This lengthens the hip flexor through the full chain to the ribcage. Hold 60s. Switch sides.

exercises.position: Kneel on the floor with your right knee down. Place your left foot flat in front of you.
exercises.next: Place your hands on your left thigh. Point both hip bones straight forward.
exercises.then: Push your hips forward until you feel a pull in your right thigh. Do not arch your lower back.
exercises.tip: Reach your right arm up to the ceiling and lean slightly to the left. Hold for 60 seconds. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Pigeon Pose - Right Hip
exercises.start: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.

steps.label: Setup
steps.cue: From tabletop, slide right knee forward behind right wrist. Extend left leg straight back. Right ankle moves toward left hip — the sharper the angle, the more intense. Important: the stretch should always be felt in the outer hip, never in the knee. If you feel any sensation in the knee joint, back the ankle closer to your body.

steps.label: Square Hips
steps.cue: Place a folded blanket or block under the right glute if the hip doesn't reach the floor. Hips stay as square as possible — do not let the pelvis tilt to one side.

steps.label: Fold Forward
steps.cue: Walk hands forward and lower chest toward the floor. Rest on forearms or forehead. Let the right hip surrender completely. Breathe into the outer right hip. Hold 90s.

exercises.position: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.
exercises.next: Slide your right knee forward behind your right hand. Slide your left leg straight back.
exercises.then: Point both hip bones forward toward the floor. Do not let your hips fall to one side.
exercises.tip: Walk your hands forward and lower your chest to the floor. Hold for 90 seconds. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Pigeon Pose - Left Hip
exercises.start: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.

steps.label: Setup
steps.cue: From tabletop, slide right knee forward behind right wrist. Extend left leg straight back. Right ankle moves toward left hip — the sharper the angle, the more intense. Important: the stretch should always be felt in the outer hip, never in the knee. If you feel any sensation in the knee joint, back the ankle closer to your body.

steps.label: Square Hips
steps.cue: Place a folded blanket or block under the right glute if the hip doesn't reach the floor. Hips stay as square as possible — do not let the pelvis tilt to one side.

steps.label: Fold Forward
steps.cue: Walk hands forward and lower chest toward the floor. Rest on forearms or forehead. Let the right hip surrender completely. Breathe into the outer right hip. Hold 90s.

exercises.position: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.
exercises.next: Slide your right knee forward behind your right hand. Slide your left leg straight back.
exercises.then: Point both hip bones forward toward the floor. Do not let your hips fall to one side.
exercises.tip: Walk your hands forward and lower your chest to the floor. Hold for 90 seconds. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Supine Figure Four - Right Hip
exercises.start: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.

steps.label: Setup
steps.cue: Lie on back, knees bent, feet flat. Cross right ankle over left knee. Flex the right foot to protect the knee joint.

steps.label: Draw In
steps.cue: Clasp hands behind left thigh and draw both legs toward chest. Feel the stretch deep in the right outer hip and glute. This targets the piriformis — the primary sciatic nerve compressor.

steps.label: Rock Gently
steps.cue: Rock slowly left and right to find the deepest point of tension. Hold 60s. Switch sides. Do not pull aggressively — let the muscle release on its own timeline.

exercises.position: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Cross your right ankle over your left knee. Pull your right toes back toward your shin.
exercises.then: Grab the back of your left thigh with both hands. Pull your left leg toward your chest.
exercises.tip: Rock slowly side to side. Hold for 60 seconds. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Supine Figure Four - Left Hip
exercises.start: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.

steps.label: Setup
steps.cue: Lie on back, knees bent, feet flat. Cross right ankle over left knee. Flex the right foot to protect the knee joint.

steps.label: Draw In
steps.cue: Clasp hands behind left thigh and draw both legs toward chest. Feel the stretch deep in the right outer hip and glute. This targets the piriformis — the primary sciatic nerve compressor.

steps.label: Rock Gently
steps.cue: Rock slowly left and right to find the deepest point of tension. Hold 60s. Switch sides. Do not pull aggressively — let the muscle release on its own timeline.

exercises.position: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Cross your right ankle over your left knee. Pull your right toes back toward your shin.
exercises.then: Grab the back of your left thigh with both hands. Pull your left leg toward your chest.
exercises.tip: Rock slowly side to side. Hold for 60 seconds. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Butterfly Stretch
exercises.start: Sit on the floor. Bring the bottoms of your feet together.

steps.label: Sit Tall
steps.cue: Sit on the floor. Bring soles of feet together, letting knees fall wide. Hold feet with both hands. Sit on a folded blanket if the lower back rounds.

steps.label: Let Knees Drop
steps.cue: Do not press the knees down. Let gravity do the work. The inner thighs and groin will release over time — not through force. Hold 60s.

steps.label: Hinge Forward
steps.cue: Hinge forward at the hips — spine long, not rounded. Walk hands forward. Feel the inner groin deepen. Hold 30s. Return upright slowly.

exercises.position: Sit on the floor. Bring the bottoms of your feet together.
exercises.next: Let your knees drop open to the sides. Hold your feet with both hands.
exercises.then: Sit tall. Let your knees sink toward the floor on their own. Do not push them down. Hold for 60 seconds.
exercises.tip: Lean your chest forward. Keep your back straight. Hold for 30 seconds.

exercises.name: Lateral Hip Opener - Right Hip
exercises.start: Lie on your right side. Stack your left leg on top of your right leg.

steps.label: Side Lie
steps.cue: Lie on your right side. Right arm extended, right ear resting on it. Stack hips and shoulders. Both knees bent at 90°.

steps.label: Open Top Knee
steps.cue: Keeping feet together, lift the left knee toward the ceiling like a clamshell opening. Do not let the pelvis roll back. Hold at the top 2s.

steps.label: Hip Circle
steps.cue: From open, draw a slow circle with the left knee — forward, down, back, up. 5 circles each direction. Feel the femoral head moving freely in the socket. Switch sides.

exercises.position: Lie on your right side. Stack your left leg on top of your right leg.
exercises.next: Bend both knees. Rest your head on your right arm. Point your hip bones straight forward.
exercises.then: Keep your feet together. Lift your left knee up toward the ceiling. Hold for 2 seconds.
exercises.tip: Move your left knee in a slow circle 5 times. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Lateral Hip Opener - Left Hip
exercises.start: Lie on your right side. Stack your left leg on top of your right leg.

steps.label: Side Lie
steps.cue: Lie on your right side. Right arm extended, right ear resting on it. Stack hips and shoulders. Both knees bent at 90°.

steps.label: Open Top Knee
steps.cue: Keeping feet together, lift the left knee toward the ceiling like a clamshell opening. Do not let the pelvis roll back. Hold at the top 2s.

steps.label: Hip Circle
steps.cue: From open, draw a slow circle with the left knee — forward, down, back, up. 5 circles each direction. Feel the femoral head moving freely in the socket. Switch sides.

exercises.position: Lie on your right side. Stack your left leg on top of your right leg.
exercises.next: Bend both knees. Rest your head on your right arm. Point your hip bones straight forward.
exercises.then: Keep your feet together. Lift your left knee up toward the ceiling. Hold for 2 seconds.
exercises.tip: Move your left knee in a slow circle 5 times. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Standing Hip Circles
exercises.start: Stand with your feet apart and hands on your hips.

steps.label: Stand Tall
steps.cue: Feet hip-width. Hands on hips. Soft knees. Eyes forward or closed. This is the integration movement — done last to bring range into a standing pattern.

steps.label: Slow Circles
steps.cue: Make large, slow circles with the pelvis — forward, right, back, left. Exaggerate the range in each direction. 8 circles clockwise.

steps.label: Reverse
steps.cue: 8 circles counter-clockwise. Move deliberately through any stiff points rather than skipping past them. The goal is a smooth, continuous arc.

exercises.position: Stand with your feet apart and hands on your hips.
exercises.next: Bend your knees slightly. Look straight ahead.
exercises.then: Push your hips forward, to the right, backward, and to the left in a large circle. Do 8 circles.
exercises.tip: Switch directions. Push your hips forward, to the left, backward, and to the right. Do 8 circles.

back.label: Lumbar Relief
back.subtitle: Decompress, Release, Strengthen
back.purpose: For days when the lower back needs care, not challenge. Gentle traction and release.

sections.label: Lower Back Soother
sections.purpose: For days when the lower back is tight, achy, or recovering. These movements decompress, not strengthen — gravity and breath do the work. Nothing here should hurt. If something does, skip it and rest in Child's Pose. This is not the session to push through.

exercises.name: Knee Hugs
exercises.start: Lie flat on your back.

steps.label: Flat on Back
steps.cue: Lie on your back. Let the whole body be heavy. Take two slow breaths before moving — this releases baseline tension before you begin.

steps.label: Hug Both Knees
steps.cue: Draw both knees to chest. Wrap arms around shins. Rock gently side to side. Feel the lower back lengthen and release against the floor.

steps.label: Hold Still
steps.cue: Find the position of most ease and hold completely still. Breathe into the lower back. Feel the lumbar vertebrae gently separate with each inhale. 60s total.

exercises.position: Lie flat on your back.
exercises.next: Let your body rest heavily on the floor. Take two slow breaths.
exercises.then: Pull both knees to your chest. Wrap your arms around your shins. Rock gently side to side.
exercises.tip: Stop rocking and hold completely still. Breathe deeply. Hold for 60 seconds.

exercises.name: Supine Pelvic Tilts
exercises.start: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.

steps.label: Lie on Back
