steps.cue: Small natural arch in lower back — this is neutral. Arms flat at sides. Do not flatten the back before you begin.

steps.label: Posterior Tilt
steps.cue: Gently press the lower back into the floor by engaging the deep abdominals. Hold 2s. The movement is subtle — this is not a crunch.

steps.label: Return to Neutral
steps.cue: Let the arch return. Move slowly and smoothly between positions. This re-educates the muscles that stabilise the lumbar spine.

exercises.position: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Rest your arms at your sides. Keep a small gap between your lower back and the floor.
exercises.then: Squeeze your stomach muscles and press your lower back flat against the floor. Hold for 2 seconds.
exercises.tip: Relax your stomach muscles and let your lower back lift again. Move slowly.

exercises.name: Cat–Cow
exercises.start: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.

steps.label: Neutral Tabletop
steps.cue: Spine neutral. Take one full breath here before moving. This is the most therapeutic movement for the lumbar spine.

steps.label: Cat — Exhale
steps.cue: Round the spine segment by segment toward the ceiling. Tuck chin and pelvis. Exhale fully. Move slowly — feel each vertebra.

steps.label: Cow — Inhale
steps.cue: Let the belly drop, lift gaze and tailbone gently. Do not compress — open. Inhale fully. 10 slow cycles. Increase the range gradually as the back warms.

exercises.position: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.
exercises.next: Place your hands under your shoulders and your knees under your hips. Keep your back flat.
exercises.then: Push your back up toward the ceiling. Tuck your chin toward your chest. Breathe out.
exercises.tip: Drop your belly toward the floor. Lift your head and tailbone toward the ceiling. Breathe in. Do 10 times.

exercises.name: Child's Pose
exercises.start: Kneel on the floor. Keep your big toes together and spread your knees wide.

steps.label: Kneel Back
steps.cue: From tabletop, sit hips back toward heels. Knees hip-width or wider. Toes together. Arms extend forward or rest alongside the body.

steps.label: Forehead Down
steps.cue: Rest forehead on floor or hands. Let the whole spine lengthen. Breathe into the back body — feel the ribcage expand outward with each inhale.

steps.label: Side Reach
steps.cue: Walk both hands to the right. Feel the left side of the body lengthen. Hold 30s. Return through center. Walk hands left. Hold 30s. Return.

exercises.position: Kneel on the floor. Keep your big toes together and spread your knees wide.
exercises.next: Sit your hips back onto your heels. Reach your arms straight out in front of you on the floor.
exercises.then: Rest your forehead on the floor. Take deep breaths into your back.
exercises.tip: Walk both hands to the right side and hold for 30 seconds. Walk both hands to the left side and hold.

exercises.name: Windshield Wipers
exercises.start: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.

steps.label: Supine Setup
steps.cue: Knees bent, feet flat and hip-width. Arms in a T, palms down. Shoulders stay grounded throughout.

steps.label: Lower Right
steps.cue: Let both knees fall slowly to the right — only as far as comfortable. Breathe out. Feel the lumbar spine rotate and release. Shoulders do not lift.

steps.label: Lower Left
steps.cue: Sweep knees back through center and lower to the left. Slow, windshield-wiper pace. This is traction for the lumbar vertebrae — gravity does the work.

exercises.position: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Reach your arms straight out to the sides. Keep your shoulders flat against the floor.
exercises.then: Let both knees drop slowly to the right side. Breathe out.
exercises.tip: Lift your knees back to the middle, then let them drop slowly to the left side.

exercises.name: Reclined Spinal Twist - Right Side
exercises.start: Lie on your back. Keep your legs straight.

steps.label: Setup
steps.cue: Lie on back. Draw right knee to chest. Arms in a T, shoulders heavy. This passively rotates the lumbar spine and releases the QL — the deepest lower back muscle.

steps.label: Cross Right
steps.cue: Guide right knee across to the left with left hand. Right arm extends, turn gaze right. Let gravity take the knee toward the floor — do not force it. Hold 60s.

steps.label: Cross Left
steps.cue: Return through center slowly. Draw left knee across to the right. Gaze left. Hold 60s. Equal time each side. The release often comes in the final 20 seconds.

exercises.position: Lie on your back. Keep your legs straight.
exercises.next: Pull your right knee to your chest. Reach your right arm straight out to the side.
exercises.then: Use your left hand to pull your right knee across your body toward the floor. Look to the right.
exercises.tip: Bring your right knee back to the middle. Switch legs and look to the left. Hold for 60 seconds each side.

exercises.name: Reclined Spinal Twist - Left Side
exercises.start: Lie on your back. Keep your legs straight.

steps.label: Setup
steps.cue: Lie on back. Draw right knee to chest. Arms in a T, shoulders heavy. This passively rotates the lumbar spine and releases the QL — the deepest lower back muscle.

steps.label: Cross Right
steps.cue: Guide right knee across to the left with left hand. Right arm extends, turn gaze right. Let gravity take the knee toward the floor — do not force it. Hold 60s.

steps.label: Cross Left
steps.cue: Return through center slowly. Draw left knee across to the right. Gaze left. Hold 60s. Equal time each side. The release often comes in the final 20 seconds.

exercises.position: Lie on your back. Keep your legs straight.
exercises.next: Pull your right knee to your chest. Reach your right arm straight out to the side.
exercises.then: Use your left hand to pull your right knee across your body toward the floor. Look to the right.
exercises.tip: Bring your right knee back to the middle. Switch legs and look to the left. Hold for 60 seconds each side.

exercises.name: Legs Up the Wall
exercises.start: Sit on the floor close to a wall.

steps.label: Setup
steps.cue: Sit sideways against a wall, then swing legs up. Hips as close to the wall as comfortable — it is fine to have a slight bend in the knees.

steps.label: Settle & Breathe
steps.cue: Let the lower back release toward the floor. Arms out at sides, palms up. Eyes closed. This drains venous pooling from the legs and decompresses the lumbar spine passively.

steps.label: Stay
steps.cue: Simply remain here. Breathe slowly. Each exhale, let the body get heavier. This is the most restorative position for a fatigued lower back. Two full minutes.

exercises.position: Sit on the floor close to a wall.
exercises.next: Lie back and swing your legs up the wall. Move your hips as close to the wall as you can.
exercises.then: Rest your arms at your sides with your palms facing up. Close your eyes.
exercises.tip: Breathe slowly. Let your body feel heavy on the floor. Stay for 2 minutes.

steady.label: Stay Steady
steady.subtitle: Stability, Balance, Coordination
steady.purpose: Train the systems that prevent falls. Simple movements that are deceptively hard to do well.

sections.label: Foundation
sections.purpose: Train the systems your body uses to prevent falls — the vestibular system, ankle strategy, and glute activation. These movements feel simple and are deceptively hard to do well. Slow down.

exercises.name: Weight Shifts
exercises.start: Stand with your feet apart. Stand near a wall if you need it for balance.

steps.label: Neutral Stance
steps.cue: Stand feet hip-width. Arms relaxed at sides. Soft knees — never locked. Eyes on a fixed point at eye level.

steps.label: Side-to-Side Shifts
steps.cue: Slowly shift your weight to the right until the left foot is nearly unloaded. Pause 2 seconds. Shift left. Use your ankles, not your hips — think of yourself as a pendulum, not a seesaw.

steps.label: Forward & Back
steps.cue: Shift weight forward toward the balls of your feet, then back toward the heels. Stay within a comfortable range. Feel the floor through your feet — this is your sensory feedback loop.

exercises.position: Stand with your feet apart. Stand near a wall if you need it for balance.
exercises.next: Keep your arms at your sides and bend your knees slightly. Look straight ahead.
exercises.then: Lean your weight slowly onto your right foot until your left foot is almost off the floor. Hold for 2 seconds.
exercises.tip: Lean your weight slowly onto your left foot. Keep your hips level.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Stance - Right Leg
exercises.start: Stand near a wall or a sturdy chair.

steps.label: Stand Tall
steps.cue: Stand near a wall or sturdy chair within arm's reach — use it for safety, not support. Find a fixed point at eye level to focus on — this is your visual anchor. Arms at sides or lightly touching the wall.

steps.label: Lift One Foot
steps.cue: Lift one foot a few inches off the floor. Keep the stance leg slightly bent — never lock the knee. The hip of the lifted leg should drop slightly, which means the standing glute med is working. Hold 20–30 seconds each side.

steps.label: Eyes Closed Progression
steps.cue: Once stable with eyes open, close them. This removes visual compensation and forces the vestibular and somatosensory systems to do the work. Even 5–10 seconds eyes-closed is a meaningful challenge.

exercises.position: Stand near a wall or a sturdy chair.
exercises.next: Rest one hand lightly on the wall. Look straight ahead.
exercises.then: Lift one foot slightly off the floor. Keep your standing knee slightly bent. Hold for 20 to 30 seconds.
exercises.tip: Switch sides. Try closing your eyes for an extra challenge.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Stance - Left Leg
exercises.start: Stand near a wall or a sturdy chair.

steps.label: Stand Tall
steps.cue: Stand near a wall or sturdy chair within arm's reach — use it for safety, not support. Find a fixed point at eye level to focus on — this is your visual anchor. Arms at sides or lightly touching the wall.

steps.label: Lift One Foot
steps.cue: Lift one foot a few inches off the floor. Keep the stance leg slightly bent — never lock the knee. The hip of the lifted leg should drop slightly, which means the standing glute med is working. Hold 20–30 seconds each side.

steps.label: Eyes Closed Progression
steps.cue: Once stable with eyes open, close them. This removes visual compensation and forces the vestibular and somatosensory systems to do the work. Even 5–10 seconds eyes-closed is a meaningful challenge.

exercises.position: Stand near a wall or a sturdy chair.
exercises.next: Rest one hand lightly on the wall. Look straight ahead.
exercises.then: Lift one foot slightly off the floor. Keep your standing knee slightly bent. Hold for 20 to 30 seconds.
exercises.tip: Switch sides. Try closing your eyes for an extra challenge.

sections.label: Dynamic Balance
sections.purpose: Moving balance is harder than standing still. These patterns train the brain to manage balance during locomotion — the context where most real-world stumbles happen.

exercises.name: Tandem Walking
exercises.start: Stand tall with a clear path in front of you.

steps.label: Starting Position
steps.cue: Stand tall. Heel of one foot touching the toes of the other. Find a line on the floor or imagine a tightrope. Arms out at sides for balance assist if needed.

steps.label: Walk Forward
steps.cue: Step forward, placing the heel of the leading foot directly against the toes of the back foot. Take 10 slow, deliberate steps forward. Eyes forward — not down at your feet.

steps.label: Walk Backward
steps.cue: Reverse direction — 10 steps back, toe touching heel. Walking backward is a separate vestibular challenge. Move slowly. This is a clinical tool used in fall prevention programs.

exercises.position: Stand tall with a clear path in front of you.
exercises.next: Reach your arms out to the sides for balance. Look straight ahead, not down at your feet.
exercises.then: Step forward. Place the heel of your front foot directly against the toes of your back foot. Take 10 steps.
exercises.tip: Take 10 steps backward. Touch your toe to your heel with each step. Move slowly.

exercises.name: Grapevine Walk
exercises.start: Stand tall with your feet together. Make sure you have space to step sideways.

steps.label: Side Start
steps.cue: Stand tall, feet together. You will move laterally. Clear enough space for 8–10 sideways steps. Arms out slightly for balance.

steps.label: Cross & Step
steps.cue: Step right with the right foot. Cross the left foot in front of the right. Step right again. Cross left foot behind the right. Repeat continuously. The alternating front/back crossover is the key pattern — it requires the brain to sequence hip abductor and adductor firing.

steps.label: Reverse Direction
steps.cue: Lead with the left foot back across the same path. Each direction uses a different motor pattern. Aim for smooth, continuous movement — choppy steps mean the brain is still learning the sequence.

exercises.position: Stand tall with your feet together. Make sure you have space to step sideways.
exercises.next: Reach your arms out slightly for balance.
exercises.then: Step your right foot to the right. Cross your left foot in front of your right foot. Step your right foot to the right again. Cross your left foot behind your right foot.
exercises.tip: Keep repeating this pattern to the right. Switch directions and step to the left.

sections.label: Strength & Control
sections.purpose: Eccentric control — the ability to decelerate your own bodyweight — is the single most important physical factor in fall prevention for adults over 40. Most injuries happen on the way down, not up.

exercises.name: Lateral Step-Downs - Right Side
exercises.start: Stand sideways on a low step or curb. Let one foot hang off the edge.

steps.label: Find Your Step
steps.cue: Stand on the edge of a bottom stair, curb, or low stool (4–6 inches high). Stand sideways so one foot is on the edge. The other foot hangs free over the side. Hold a wall lightly for safety — not for support.

steps.label: Slow Descent
steps.cue: Bend the standing leg to slowly lower the hanging foot toward the floor. Take 3–4 full seconds to descend. The standing knee should track directly over the second toe — not collapsing inward. This slow lowering is the entire point: you are training the quad to control deceleration.

steps.label: Return to Top
steps.cue: Before the hanging foot touches the floor, reverse direction and press back to standing. Do not push off the lower foot — all the work comes from the standing leg. Switch sides after all reps.

exercises.position: Stand sideways on a low step or curb. Let one foot hang off the edge.
exercises.next: Rest one hand lightly on a wall for balance.
exercises.then: Bend your standing knee slowly. Lower your hanging foot toward the floor. Take 3 to 4 seconds to go down.
exercises.tip: Straighten your standing leg to push back up. Do not push off the floor with your bottom foot. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Lateral Step-Downs - Left Side
exercises.start: Stand sideways on a low step or curb. Let one foot hang off the edge.

steps.label: Find Your Step
steps.cue: Stand on the edge of a bottom stair, curb, or low stool (4–6 inches high). Stand sideways so one foot is on the edge. The other foot hangs free over the side. Hold a wall lightly for safety — not for support.

steps.label: Slow Descent
steps.cue: Bend the standing leg to slowly lower the hanging foot toward the floor. Take 3–4 full seconds to descend. The standing knee should track directly over the second toe — not collapsing inward. This slow lowering is the entire point: you are training the quad to control deceleration.

steps.label: Return to Top
steps.cue: Before the hanging foot touches the floor, reverse direction and press back to standing. Do not push off the lower foot — all the work comes from the standing leg. Switch sides after all reps.

exercises.position: Stand sideways on a low step or curb. Let one foot hang off the edge.
exercises.next: Rest one hand lightly on a wall for balance.
exercises.then: Bend your standing knee slowly. Lower your hanging foot toward the floor. Take 3 to 4 seconds to go down.
exercises.tip: Straighten your standing leg to push back up. Do not push off the floor with your bottom foot. Switch sides.

freely.label: Joint Glide
freely.subtitle: CARS, Mobility, Freedom
freely.purpose: Controlled Articular Rotations and mobility work for every major joint.

sections.label: Spine
sections.purpose: Most adults over 40 have lost thoracic extension without knowing it. This single deficit drives frozen shoulder, neck strain, and lower back compensation. Address it directly.

exercises.name: Thoracic Extension
exercises.start: Sit at the edge of a firm chair.

steps.label: Chair Setup
steps.cue: Sit at the edge of a firm chair. Place both hands behind your head, fingers interlaced, supporting the weight of your head. Identify the middle of your back — the thoracic spine, roughly between your shoulder blades. This is the target.

steps.label: Arch Back
steps.cue: Lean back so the top edge of the chair back contacts your mid-spine. Let the upper back gently arch over the support. Do not extend the lower back or neck — this is a thoracic-only movement. Breathe out as you arch. Feel the front of the chest opening.

steps.label: Segment & Breathe
steps.cue: Hold for 2–3 breaths at each level. Then shift slightly up or down the chair back to target adjacent segments. 3–5 positions across the thoracic spine. Never force — the weight of your head is sufficient traction.

exercises.position: Sit at the edge of a firm chair.
exercises.next: Clasp your hands behind your head. Lean back so the top of the chair presses into the middle of your back.
exercises.then: Lean your upper body back over the top of the chair. Breathe out. Keep your lower back and neck straight.
exercises.tip: Move your hips slightly forward or backward on the chair to stretch a different part of your back.

sections.label: Upper Body
sections.purpose: CARS — Controlled Articular Rotations — are the gold standard for joint health. Each rep moves the joint through its full available range under active muscular tension. Think: moving through thick honey. No momentum. The resistance comes entirely from you.

exercises.name: Neck CARs
exercises.start: Sit or stand tall.

steps.label: Setup & Brace
steps.cue: Sit or stand tall. Pack the shoulders down away from ears. Engage the muscles of the neck lightly — imagine you are trying to make a double chin. This pre-tension is what makes CARs different from a regular neck stretch: the joint moves, the surrounding tissues stay engaged.

steps.label: The Half-Arc
steps.cue: Begin by drawing your chin toward your chest. Slowly arc the head to the right — ear toward right shoulder, continuing the arc until the ear moves toward the left shoulder, then chin returns to chest. One full half-arc takes 8–10 seconds. Move as if carving through resistance. Never tilt the head back — the arc travels chin to chest only, never through the back of the neck.

steps.label: Reverse & Repeat
steps.cue: At any point where you feel stiffness or reduced range, slow down further and breathe into that position. Do not push through pain or any sharp sensation. Reverse direction for one full arc. 2–3 rotations each direction is sufficient. If you feel dizziness at any point, stop immediately and rest.

exercises.position: Sit or stand tall.
exercises.next: Relax your shoulders down. Pull your chin straight back slightly.
exercises.then: Drop your chin to your chest. Slowly roll your right ear toward your right shoulder, then your left ear toward your left shoulder, and back to your chest.
exercises.tip: Move very slowly. Do not lean your head backward. Switch directions.

exercises.name: Shoulder CARs
exercises.start: Stand tall with your arms at your sides.

steps.label: Setup & Lock Down
steps.cue: Stand tall. Work one arm at a time. Before moving, engage the lat on the working side — imagine trying to crush a walnut in your armpit. This stabilizes the scapula so the ball-and-socket joint does the work, not the whole shoulder blade.

steps.label: Full Arc Forward
steps.cue: Keeping the arm straight and under tension, lift it forward and up overhead in a large circle. As it passes overhead, rotate the palm to face behind you, then continue the circle down behind your back as far as your range allows. Move through thick resistance. The entire arc should take 10–12 seconds.

steps.label: Reverse Arc
steps.cue: At the limit of your range behind the body, pause and breathe. Reverse the circle — same path, same resistance, same slow pace. Switch arms after 2 full rotations. The shoulder that feels stuck is the one that needs this most.

exercises.position: Stand tall with your arms at your sides.
exercises.next: Squeeze the muscles under your right armpit. Keep your right arm perfectly straight.
exercises.then: Lift your right arm straight in front of you and up to the ceiling. Turn your palm to face backward, then reach your arm behind you and down to your side in a large circle.
exercises.tip: Move very slowly. Reverse the circle. Switch arms.

exercises.name: Wrist CARs
exercises.start: Sit or stand. Reach both arms straight out in front of you.

steps.label: Setup
steps.cue: Sit or stand. Extend both arms in front at shoulder height. Make loose fists. Engage the forearm muscles lightly — you should feel mild tension in the forearm, not the fingers. This active tension is what makes this therapeutic rather than passive.

steps.label: Full Rotation
steps.cue: Slowly rotate both wrists simultaneously through their full available range: flex down, rotate toward the little finger side, extend back, rotate toward the thumb side. One full circle takes 6–8 seconds. Move as if drawing large circles in the air with your knuckles.

steps.label: Reverse & Isolate
steps.cue: Reverse direction for equal rotations. If one wrist has less range in a particular direction, slow down there — that is where the synovial fluid is most restricted. 4–5 rotations each direction is a complete set.

exercises.position: Sit or stand. Reach both arms straight out in front of you.
exercises.next: Make loose fists with both hands. Keep your arms completely still.
exercises.then: Move your fists in slow, large circles. Bend your wrists down, to the side, up, and to the other side.
exercises.tip: Switch directions. Move very slowly.

sections.label: Lower Body
sections.purpose: The hip and ankle are the most neglected joints for adults who sit. Synovial fluid only reaches cartilage during movement — compression from sitting drives it out. These rotations restore joint space and end-range control.

exercises.name: Hip CARs - Right Hip
exercises.start: Stand on one leg. Rest one hand lightly on a wall.

steps.label: Setup & Stabilize
steps.cue: Stand on one leg. Hold a wall lightly with one hand for safety only — not for balance support. Engage the standing glute to keep the pelvis completely still. The entire point of Hip CARs is that the pelvis does not move — only the femur in the hip socket. Squeeze the core before lifting the leg.

steps.label: Full Circumduction
steps.cue: Lift the free knee and begin rotating the hip: knee lifts forward, swings out to the side, extends behind the body, sweeps back to center. One full rotation takes 8–10 seconds. Keep the circle as large as your range allows while the pelvis stays completely still. If the hip hikes or the lower back twists, you have exceeded your available range — make the circle smaller.

steps.label: Reverse & Switch
steps.cue: Reverse direction for the same number of rotations. At any restricted point in the arc, slow further and breathe. 2–3 rotations each direction, each side. The hip that feels grinding or clicking is the one that needs the most consistent attention.

exercises.position: Stand on one leg. Rest one hand lightly on a wall.
exercises.next: Keep your body completely still. Squeeze your stomach muscles.
exercises.then: Lift your free knee up. Move it out to the side, reach it backward, and bring it back to the middle in a large circle.
exercises.tip: Switch directions. Move very slowly. Switch legs.

exercises.name: Hip CARs - Left Hip
exercises.start: Stand on one leg. Rest one hand lightly on a wall.

steps.label: Setup & Stabilize
steps.cue: Stand on one leg. Hold a wall lightly with one hand for safety only — not for balance support. Engage the standing glute to keep the pelvis completely still. The entire point of Hip CARs is that the pelvis does not move — only the femur in the hip socket. Squeeze the core before lifting the leg.

steps.label: Full Circumduction
steps.cue: Lift the free knee and begin rotating the hip: knee lifts forward, swings out to the side, extends behind the body, sweeps back to center. One full rotation takes 8–10 seconds. Keep the circle as large as your range allows while the pelvis stays completely still. If the hip hikes or the lower back twists, you have exceeded your available range — make the circle smaller.

steps.label: Reverse & Switch
steps.cue: Reverse direction for the same number of rotations. At any restricted point in the arc, slow further and breathe. 2–3 rotations each direction, each side. The hip that feels grinding or clicking is the one that needs the most consistent attention.

exercises.position: Stand on one leg. Rest one hand lightly on a wall.
exercises.next: Keep your body completely still. Squeeze your stomach muscles.
exercises.then: Lift your free knee up. Move it out to the side, reach it backward, and bring it back to the middle in a large circle.
exercises.tip: Switch directions. Move very slowly. Switch legs.

exercises.name: Ankle CARs
exercises.start: Sit in a chair. Lift one foot off the floor.

steps.label: Seated Setup
steps.cue: Sit in a chair. Cross one ankle over the opposite knee so the foot is free in the air. Hold the lower leg just above the ankle to stabilize the shin — you want the foot to move, not the whole leg. Engage the muscles of the lower leg lightly.

steps.label: Full Rotation
steps.cue: Draw the largest circle possible with your big toe. Move clockwise: point the foot forward, rotate out, flex back, rotate in. Take 5–6 seconds per full rotation. Most people discover significant restriction when moving into dorsiflexion (toes pulled back) — this is the direction that matters most for walking and balance.

steps.label: Reverse & Switch
steps.cue: Reverse direction. 4–5 rotations each way per foot. If the ankle feels stiff or crunchy, this is synovial fluid restriction — not a reason to stop, but a reason to go slower and breathe through the range.

exercises.position: Sit in a chair. Lift one foot off the floor.
exercises.next: Grab your leg just above your ankle with both hands. Keep your leg completely still.
exercises.then: Draw the largest circle you can with your big toe. Move very slowly.
exercises.tip: Switch directions. Switch feet.

exercises.name: Adductor Rock-Back - Right Side
exercises.start: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.
exercises.sub: Posterior hip capsule & inner thigh release

steps.label: Quadruped Setup
steps.cue: Begin on hands and knees. Hands under shoulders, knees under hips. Spine neutral. Extend one leg directly out to the side — keep it straight, toes pointing forward or slightly down. The extended leg forms a 90-degree angle with your body.

steps.label: Rock Back
steps.cue: Slowly shift your hips back toward your heels, keeping the extended leg in place. You will feel a deep stretch along the inner thigh and the back of the hip on the extended side. Take 3–4 seconds to rock back. Breathe out during the descent. Do not round the lower back.

steps.label: Rock Forward & Repeat
steps.cue: Rock forward to the starting position. Pause briefly, then rock back again. 8–10 slow repetitions, then switch sides. This movement targets the posterior hip capsule and adductors — two structures that chronic sitting locks into permanent shortening.

exercises.position: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.
exercises.next: Reach one leg straight out to the side. Point your toes straight forward. Keep your back flat.
exercises.then: Push your hips slowly backward toward your heel. Breathe out as you go down.
exercises.tip: Pull your hips forward to start again. Do 8 to 10 times, then switch sides.

exercises.name: Adductor Rock-Back - Left Side
exercises.start: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.
exercises.sub: Posterior hip capsule & inner thigh release

steps.label: Quadruped Setup
steps.cue: Begin on hands and knees. Hands under shoulders, knees under hips. Spine neutral. Extend one leg directly out to the side — keep it straight, toes pointing forward or slightly down. The extended leg forms a 90-degree angle with your body.

steps.label: Rock Back
steps.cue: Slowly shift your hips back toward your heels, keeping the extended leg in place. You will feel a deep stretch along the inner thigh and the back of the hip on the extended side. Take 3–4 seconds to rock back. Breathe out during the descent. Do not round the lower back.

steps.label: Rock Forward & Repeat
steps.cue: Rock forward to the starting position. Pause briefly, then rock back again. 8–10 slow repetitions, then switch sides. This movement targets the posterior hip capsule and adductors — two structures that chronic sitting locks into permanent shortening.

exercises.position: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.
exercises.next: Reach one leg straight out to the side. Point your toes straight forward. Keep your back flat.
exercises.then: Push your hips slowly backward toward your heel. Breathe out as you go down.
exercises.tip: Pull your hips forward to start again. Do 8 to 10 times, then switch sides.

transit.label: In Transit
transit.subtitle: Seated, Circulation, Calm
transit.purpose: Four exercises designed for your seat. Keep circulation moving and the nervous system calm during any long journey.

sections.label: In-Transit
sections.purpose: Designed for a seat. Zero equipment, minimal space. These four movements keep circulation moving, prevent stiffness, and down-regulate the nervous system during long flights or drives.

exercises.name: Ankle Pumps & Circles
exercises.start: Sit tall with your feet flat on the floor.
exercises.sub: Circulation — prevents swelling and blood pooling

steps.label: Seated Start
steps.cue: Sit upright, feet flat on the floor. Lift both feet slightly so heels rest lightly. No need to remove shoes.

steps.label: Pump Up & Down
steps.cue: Flex feet up (toes toward shins) then point down — 20 slow repetitions. This activates the soleus muscle, which acts as a second heart to pump blood back from the legs.

steps.label: Slow Circles
steps.cue: Rotate each ankle in slow, full circles — 10 clockwise, 10 counter-clockwise per foot. Imagine drawing large, smooth circles in the air with your big toe.

exercises.position: Sit tall with your feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Lift your feet slightly so only your heels touch the floor.
exercises.then: Pull your toes up toward your shins, then point them down toward the floor. Do this 20 times.
exercises.tip: Move your ankles in slow, large circles 10 times. Switch directions.

exercises.name: Seated Glute Squeezes
exercises.start: Sit tall with your feet flat on the floor.
exercises.sub: Prevents gluteal amnesia from prolonged pressure

steps.label: Neutral Sit
steps.cue: Sit tall, feet flat. No leaning — weight distributed evenly through both sit bones. Shoulders relaxed.

steps.label: Squeeze & Hold
steps.cue: Squeeze both glutes firmly together. Hold for 5 seconds. Breathe normally — do not hold your breath or brace the upper body.

steps.label: Release & Repeat
steps.cue: Release completely for 5 seconds. 10 repetitions. Prolonged pressure switches off the glutes over time — this keeps the tissue and motor signal alive.

exercises.position: Sit tall with your feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Relax your shoulders. Keep your weight even on both hips.
exercises.then: Squeeze your butt muscles firmly. Hold for 5 seconds. Keep breathing.
exercises.tip: Release completely for 5 seconds. Repeat 10 times.

exercises.name: Seated Cat-Cow
exercises.start: Sit at the front edge of your seat with your hands on your knees.
exercises.sub: Lubricates intervertebral discs compressed under cabin pressure

steps.label: Hands on Knees
steps.cue: Sit at the front third of your seat. Hands resting on knees. Spine neutral. Take one breath here.

steps.label: Arch — Inhale
steps.cue: Inhale as you arch the low back, lift the chest, and gently look upward. Let the spine extend segmentally from tailbone to neck.

steps.label: Round — Exhale
steps.cue: Exhale fully as you round the spine, tuck the chin, and pull the navel in. Feel each vertebra flex. 8–10 slow cycles.

exercises.position: Sit at the front edge of your seat with your hands on your knees.
exercises.next: Keep your back straight. Take a deep breath.
exercises.then: Arch your back, lift your chest, and look slightly up. Breathe in.
exercises.tip: Round your back, tuck your chin, and pull your belly in. Breathe out. Repeat 8 to 10 times.

exercises.name: Peripheral Vision Softening
exercises.start: Sit tall and look straight ahead.
exercises.sub: Down-regulates the nervous system during stress or turbulence

steps.label: Soft Gaze Forward
steps.cue: Let your eyes relax and unfocus slightly. Do not look at a screen or read. Allow the gaze to become wide and diffuse.

steps.label: Expand Peripheral Awareness
steps.cue: Without moving your eyes, softly notice the full width of your visual field — the edges of the cabin, movement in your periphery. This is panoramic vision, the same visual state predators use when resting.

steps.label: Hold & Breathe
steps.cue: Maintain this soft, wide gaze for 60–90 seconds. Breathe slowly. This activates the parasympathetic nervous system and reduces the threat-detection response that makes turbulence feel dangerous.

exercises.position: Sit tall and look straight ahead.
exercises.next: Let your eyes relax. Do not look at a screen or read.
exercises.then: Keep your eyes still, but notice the space and movement out of the corners of your eyes.
exercises.tip: Hold this soft gaze for 60 to 90 seconds. Breathe slowly.

sections.label: Post-Arrival
sections.purpose: Do these as soon as you reach your hotel or home. They undo the posture of travel — compressed hip flexors, locked thoracic spine, forward shoulders — and signal the body that you have arrived.

exercises.name: Standing Psoas Release - Right Side
exercises.start: Stand tall and take a small step forward.
exercises.sub: Long-axis stretch for hip flexors shortened during travel

steps.label: Shallow Lunge
steps.cue: Step forward into a shallow lunge — not a deep split. Back knee can be raised or lowered. Both feet point forward. Keep torso upright.

steps.label: Pelvic Tuck & Lean
steps.cue: Tuck the pelvis slightly (posterior tilt) to flatten the low back. Gently lean the torso toward the front leg. You will feel a deep stretch at the front of the back hip — this is the psoas.

steps.label: Hold & Breathe
steps.cue: Hold 30–45 seconds each side. With each exhale, allow the hip to sink a little further. Do not force — the psoas responds to patience, not pressure.

exercises.position: Stand tall and take a small step forward.
exercises.next: Keep both feet pointing forward. Keep your chest up.
exercises.then: Tuck your tailbone slightly under. Lean your chest gently forward until you feel a pull in your back hip.
exercises.tip: Hold for 30 to 45 seconds. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Standing Psoas Release - Left Side
exercises.start: Stand tall and take a small step forward.
exercises.sub: Long-axis stretch for hip flexors shortened during travel

steps.label: Shallow Lunge
steps.cue: Step forward into a shallow lunge — not a deep split. Back knee can be raised or lowered. Both feet point forward. Keep torso upright.

steps.label: Pelvic Tuck & Lean
steps.cue: Tuck the pelvis slightly (posterior tilt) to flatten the low back. Gently lean the torso toward the front leg. You will feel a deep stretch at the front of the back hip — this is the psoas.

steps.label: Hold & Breathe
steps.cue: Hold 30–45 seconds each side. With each exhale, allow the hip to sink a little further. Do not force — the psoas responds to patience, not pressure.

exercises.position: Stand tall and take a small step forward.
exercises.next: Keep both feet pointing forward. Keep your chest up.
exercises.then: Tuck your tailbone slightly under. Lean your chest gently forward until you feel a pull in your back hip.
exercises.tip: Hold for 30 to 45 seconds. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Wall Slides (W to Y)
exercises.start: Stand with your back flat against a wall.
exercises.sub: Activates lower trapezius to fix 'luggage shoulder'

steps.label: Wall Contact
steps.cue: Stand with head, upper back, and glutes touching the wall. Arms in a W shape — elbows bent, backs of hands against the wall. This is your baseline.

steps.label: Slide to Y
steps.cue: Keeping the backs of hands and elbows touching the wall, slowly slide arms upward until they form a Y overhead. If contact is lost, reduce range. The lower trapezius must fire to keep the shoulder blades down and in.

steps.label: Slow Return
steps.cue: Lower arms back to W in 3 seconds. Do not shrug or let shoulders elevate. 10 reps. This directly reverses the upper trap dominance that carrying luggage creates.

exercises.position: Stand with your back flat against a wall.
exercises.next: Press your head, upper back, and hips into the wall. Bend your elbows and press the backs of your hands against the wall in a W shape.
exercises.then: Slide your arms slowly up the wall until they form a Y shape above your head. Keep your hands on the wall.
exercises.tip: Slide your arms back down to a W shape. Do 10 times.

exercises.name: Open Book Stretch - Right Side
exercises.start: Lie on your side with your knees bent and stacked.
exercises.sub: Restores thoracic rotation — the first thing to lock after travel

steps.label: Side-Lying Setup
steps.cue: Lie on your side with hips and knees bent to 90°. Stack knees together. Extend both arms forward at shoulder height. Place top hand on top knee to keep hips anchored.

steps.label: Open the Top Arm
steps.cue: Keeping the bottom knee stack stable, slowly rotate the top arm up, back, and toward the floor behind you — like opening a book. Let the chest and thoracic spine follow the arm.

steps.label: Hold & Return
steps.cue: Hold for 3–5 breaths at the end range. Do not force the shoulder to the floor — let gravity do it gradually. Return slowly. 5 reps each side.

exercises.position: Lie on your side with your knees bent and stacked.
exercises.next: Reach both arms straight out in front of you. Keep your knees glued together.
exercises.then: Lift your top arm. Reach it up and over your body toward the floor behind you. Follow your hand with your eyes.
exercises.tip: Hold for 3 to 5 breaths. Slowly bring your arm back to the start. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Open Book Stretch - Left Side
exercises.start: Lie on your side with your knees bent and stacked.
exercises.sub: Restores thoracic rotation — the first thing to lock after travel

steps.label: Side-Lying Setup
steps.cue: Lie on your side with hips and knees bent to 90°. Stack knees together. Extend both arms forward at shoulder height. Place top hand on top knee to keep hips anchored.

steps.label: Open the Top Arm
steps.cue: Keeping the bottom knee stack stable, slowly rotate the top arm up, back, and toward the floor behind you — like opening a book. Let the chest and thoracic spine follow the arm.

steps.label: Hold & Return
steps.cue: Hold for 3–5 breaths at the end range. Do not force the shoulder to the floor — let gravity do it gradually. Return slowly. 5 reps each side.

exercises.position: Lie on your side with your knees bent and stacked.
exercises.next: Reach both arms straight out in front of you. Keep your knees glued together.
exercises.then: Lift your top arm. Reach it up and over your body toward the floor behind you. Follow your hand with your eyes.
exercises.tip: Hold for 3 to 5 breaths. Slowly bring your arm back to the start. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Physiological Sigh
exercises.start: Sit or lie down in a comfortable position.
exercises.sub: Rapidly offloads CO₂ and signals the brain you have arrived safely

steps.label: Relax Completely
steps.cue: Sit or lie comfortably. Let the body be heavy. Close the eyes. This is not just breathing — it is a signal to the nervous system that the travel phase is over.

steps.label: Double Inhale
steps.cue: Breathe in through the nose to about 80% capacity, then sniff in a second short inhale to fully inflate the lungs. This pops collapsed air sacs (alveoli) and maximizes surface area for CO₂ exchange.

steps.label: Long Exhale
steps.cue: Release a long, slow exhale through the mouth — twice as long as the inhale. 3–5 rounds. Each cycle measurably reduces physiological arousal. You will feel the nervous system shift.

exercises.position: Sit or lie down in a comfortable position.
exercises.next: Close your eyes. Let your body feel heavy.
exercises.then: Breathe deeply in through your nose, then take one more quick sniff through your nose to fill your lungs completely.
exercises.tip: Breathe out slowly through your mouth. Make your breath out twice as long as your breath in. Do 3 to 5 times.

exercises.name: Doorway Pec Stretch
exercises.start: Stand in a doorway.
exercises.sub: Counteracts internal shoulder rotation from carrying bags

steps.label: Doorway Setup
steps.cue: Stand in a doorway. Place both forearms on the door frame at 90° — elbows at shoulder height, upper arms parallel to floor. Step one foot forward.

steps.label: Lean Through
steps.cue: Gently shift your weight forward through the doorway. Let the chest open. You should feel a stretch across the pectorals and front of the shoulders.

steps.label: Hold & Breathe
steps.cue: Hold 30–45 seconds. On each exhale, allow the chest to open a fraction more. Do not shrug the shoulders up. Switch foot position halfway through.

exercises.position: Stand in a doorway.
exercises.next: Place both forearms on the door frame. Keep your elbows bent at shoulder height. Step one foot forward.
exercises.then: Lean your chest gently forward through the doorway.
exercises.tip: Hold for 30 to 45 seconds. Keep your shoulders relaxed down.

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
exercises.start: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
exercises.sub: Re-engages the posterior chain after hours of sitting

steps.label: Supine Setup
steps.cue: Lie on your back. Feet flat, hip-width, heels 6 inches from glutes. Arms flat at sides. This is your reset position after travel.

steps.label: Drive & Lift
steps.cue: Press through the heels and lift the hips until shoulders, hips, and knees form a straight line. Squeeze the glutes firmly at the top. Hold 2 seconds.

steps.label: Lower Slowly
steps.cue: Lower with control — 3 seconds down. Do not let the hips crash. The slow descent builds the posterior chain that sitting disengages.

exercises.position: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Keep your feet hip-width apart. Rest your arms flat at your sides.
exercises.then: Push your heels into the floor. Lift your hips until your body makes a straight line from your shoulders to your knees. Squeeze your butt at the top.
exercises.tip: Lower your hips slowly back to the floor. Take 3 seconds to go down.

travel.label: Arrival Unwind
travel.subtitle: Travel, Decompress, Reset
travel.purpose: Six movements to undo the posture of travel. Do these as soon as you arrive.

sections.label: In-Transit
sections.purpose: Designed for a seat. Zero equipment, minimal space. These four movements keep circulation moving, prevent stiffness, and down-regulate the nervous system during long flights or drives.

exercises.name: Ankle Pumps & Circles
exercises.start: Sit tall with your feet flat on the floor.
exercises.sub: Circulation — prevents swelling and blood pooling

steps.label: Seated Start
steps.cue: Sit upright, feet flat on the floor. Lift both feet slightly so heels rest lightly. No need to remove shoes.

steps.label: Pump Up & Down
steps.cue: Flex feet up (toes toward shins) then point down — 20 slow repetitions. This activates the soleus muscle, which acts as a second heart to pump blood back from the legs.

steps.label: Slow Circles
steps.cue: Rotate each ankle in slow, full circles — 10 clockwise, 10 counter-clockwise per foot. Imagine drawing large, smooth circles in the air with your big toe.

exercises.position: Sit tall with your feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Lift your feet slightly so only your heels touch the floor.
exercises.then: Pull your toes up toward your shins, then point them down toward the floor. Do this 20 times.
exercises.tip: Move your ankles in slow, large circles 10 times. Switch directions.

exercises.name: Seated Glute Squeezes
exercises.start: Sit tall with your feet flat on the floor.
exercises.sub: Prevents gluteal amnesia from prolonged pressure

steps.label: Neutral Sit
steps.cue: Sit tall, feet flat. No leaning — weight distributed evenly through both sit bones. Shoulders relaxed.

steps.label: Squeeze & Hold
steps.cue: Squeeze both glutes firmly together. Hold for 5 seconds. Breathe normally — do not hold your breath or brace the upper body.

steps.label: Release & Repeat
steps.cue: Release completely for 5 seconds. 10 repetitions. Prolonged pressure switches off the glutes over time — this keeps the tissue and motor signal alive.

exercises.position: Sit tall with your feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Relax your shoulders. Keep your weight even on both hips.
exercises.then: Squeeze your butt muscles firmly. Hold for 5 seconds. Keep breathing.
exercises.tip: Release completely for 5 seconds. Repeat 10 times.

exercises.name: Seated Cat-Cow
exercises.start: Sit at the front edge of your seat with your hands on your knees.
exercises.sub: Lubricates intervertebral discs compressed under cabin pressure

steps.label: Hands on Knees
steps.cue: Sit at the front third of your seat. Hands resting on knees. Spine neutral. Take one breath here.

steps.label: Arch — Inhale
steps.cue: Inhale as you arch the low back, lift the chest, and gently look upward. Let the spine extend segmentally from tailbone to neck.

steps.label: Round — Exhale
steps.cue: Exhale fully as you round the spine, tuck the chin, and pull the navel in. Feel each vertebra flex. 8–10 slow cycles.

exercises.position: Sit at the front edge of your seat with your hands on your knees.
exercises.next: Keep your back straight. Take a deep breath.
exercises.then: Arch your back, lift your chest, and look slightly up. Breathe in.
exercises.tip: Round your back, tuck your chin, and pull your belly in. Breathe out. Repeat 8 to 10 times.

exercises.name: Peripheral Vision Softening
exercises.start: Sit tall and look straight ahead.
exercises.sub: Down-regulates the nervous system during stress or turbulence

steps.label: Soft Gaze Forward
steps.cue: Let your eyes relax and unfocus slightly. Do not look at a screen or read. Allow the gaze to become wide and diffuse.

steps.label: Expand Peripheral Awareness
steps.cue: Without moving your eyes, softly notice the full width of your visual field — the edges of the cabin, movement in your periphery. This is panoramic vision, the same visual state predators use when resting.

steps.label: Hold & Breathe
steps.cue: Maintain this soft, wide gaze for 60–90 seconds. Breathe slowly. This activates the parasympathetic nervous system and reduces the threat-detection response that makes turbulence feel dangerous.

exercises.position: Sit tall and look straight ahead.
exercises.next: Let your eyes relax. Do not look at a screen or read.
exercises.then: Keep your eyes still, but notice the space and movement out of the corners of your eyes.
exercises.tip: Hold this soft gaze for 60 to 90 seconds. Breathe slowly.

sections.label: Post-Arrival
sections.purpose: Do these as soon as you reach your hotel or home. They undo the posture of travel — compressed hip flexors, locked thoracic spine, forward shoulders — and signal the body that you have arrived.

exercises.name: Standing Psoas Release - Right Side
exercises.start: Stand tall and take a small step forward.
exercises.sub: Long-axis stretch for hip flexors shortened during travel

steps.label: Shallow Lunge
steps.cue: Step forward into a shallow lunge — not a deep split. Back knee can be raised or lowered. Both feet point forward. Keep torso upright.

steps.label: Pelvic Tuck & Lean
steps.cue: Tuck the pelvis slightly (posterior tilt) to flatten the low back. Gently lean the torso toward the front leg. You will feel a deep stretch at the front of the back hip — this is the psoas.

steps.label: Hold & Breathe
steps.cue: Hold 30–45 seconds each side. With each exhale, allow the hip to sink a little further. Do not force — the psoas responds to patience, not pressure.

exercises.position: Stand tall and take a small step forward.
exercises.next: Keep both feet pointing forward. Keep your chest up.
exercises.then: Tuck your tailbone slightly under. Lean your chest gently forward until you feel a pull in your back hip.
exercises.tip: Hold for 30 to 45 seconds. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Standing Psoas Release - Left Side
exercises.start: Stand tall and take a small step forward.
exercises.sub: Long-axis stretch for hip flexors shortened during travel

steps.label: Shallow Lunge
steps.cue: Step forward into a shallow lunge — not a deep split. Back knee can be raised or lowered. Both feet point forward. Keep torso upright.

steps.label: Pelvic Tuck & Lean
steps.cue: Tuck the pelvis slightly (posterior tilt) to flatten the low back. Gently lean the torso toward the front leg. You will feel a deep stretch at the front of the back hip — this is the psoas.

steps.label: Hold & Breathe
steps.cue: Hold 30–45 seconds each side. With each exhale, allow the hip to sink a little further. Do not force — the psoas responds to patience, not pressure.

exercises.position: Stand tall and take a small step forward.
exercises.next: Keep both feet pointing forward. Keep your chest up.
exercises.then: Tuck your tailbone slightly under. Lean your chest gently forward until you feel a pull in your back hip.
exercises.tip: Hold for 30 to 45 seconds. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Wall Slides (W to Y)
exercises.start: Stand with your back flat against a wall.
exercises.sub: Activates lower trapezius to fix 'luggage shoulder'

steps.label: Wall Contact
steps.cue: Stand with head, upper back, and glutes touching the wall. Arms in a W shape — elbows bent, backs of hands against the wall. This is your baseline.

steps.label: Slide to Y
steps.cue: Keeping the backs of hands and elbows touching the wall, slowly slide arms upward until they form a Y overhead. If contact is lost, reduce range. The lower trapezius must fire to keep the shoulder blades down and in.

steps.label: Slow Return
steps.cue: Lower arms back to W in 3 seconds. Do not shrug or let shoulders elevate. 10 reps. This directly reverses the upper trap dominance that carrying luggage creates.

exercises.position: Stand with your back flat against a wall.
exercises.next: Press your head, upper back, and hips into the wall. Bend your elbows and press the backs of your hands against the wall in a W shape.
exercises.then: Slide your arms slowly up the wall until they form a Y shape above your head. Keep your hands on the wall.
exercises.tip: Slide your arms back down to a W shape. Do 10 times.

exercises.name: Open Book Stretch - Right Side
exercises.start: Lie on your side with your knees bent and stacked.
exercises.sub: Restores thoracic rotation — the first thing to lock after travel

steps.label: Side-Lying Setup
steps.cue: Lie on your side with hips and knees bent to 90°. Stack knees together. Extend both arms forward at shoulder height. Place top hand on top knee to keep hips anchored.

steps.label: Open the Top Arm
steps.cue: Keeping the bottom knee stack stable, slowly rotate the top arm up, back, and toward the floor behind you — like opening a book. Let the chest and thoracic spine follow the arm.

steps.label: Hold & Return
steps.cue: Hold for 3–5 breaths at the end range. Do not force the shoulder to the floor — let gravity do it gradually. Return slowly. 5 reps each side.

exercises.position: Lie on your side with your knees bent and stacked.
exercises.next: Reach both arms straight out in front of you. Keep your knees glued together.
exercises.then: Lift your top arm. Reach it up and over your body toward the floor behind you. Follow your hand with your eyes.
exercises.tip: Hold for 3 to 5 breaths. Slowly bring your arm back to the start. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Open Book Stretch - Left Side
exercises.start: Lie on your side with your knees bent and stacked.
exercises.sub: Restores thoracic rotation — the first thing to lock after travel

steps.label: Side-Lying Setup
steps.cue: Lie on your side with hips and knees bent to 90°. Stack knees together. Extend both arms forward at shoulder height. Place top hand on top knee to keep hips anchored.

steps.label: Open the Top Arm
steps.cue: Keeping the bottom knee stack stable, slowly rotate the top arm up, back, and toward the floor behind you — like opening a book. Let the chest and thoracic spine follow the arm.

steps.label: Hold & Return
steps.cue: Hold for 3–5 breaths at the end range. Do not force the shoulder to the floor — let gravity do it gradually. Return slowly. 5 reps each side.

exercises.position: Lie on your side with your knees bent and stacked.
exercises.next: Reach both arms straight out in front of you. Keep your knees glued together.
exercises.then: Lift your top arm. Reach it up and over your body toward the floor behind you. Follow your hand with your eyes.
exercises.tip: Hold for 3 to 5 breaths. Slowly bring your arm back to the start. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Physiological Sigh
exercises.start: Sit or lie down in a comfortable position.
exercises.sub: Rapidly offloads CO₂ and signals the brain you have arrived safely

steps.label: Relax Completely
steps.cue: Sit or lie comfortably. Let the body be heavy. Close the eyes. This is not just breathing — it is a signal to the nervous system that the travel phase is over.

steps.label: Double Inhale
steps.cue: Breathe in through the nose to about 80% capacity, then sniff in a second short inhale to fully inflate the lungs. This pops collapsed air sacs (alveoli) and maximizes surface area for CO₂ exchange.

steps.label: Long Exhale
steps.cue: Release a long, slow exhale through the mouth — twice as long as the inhale. 3–5 rounds. Each cycle measurably reduces physiological arousal. You will feel the nervous system shift.

exercises.position: Sit or lie down in a comfortable position.
exercises.next: Close your eyes. Let your body feel heavy.
exercises.then: Breathe deeply in through your nose, then take one more quick sniff through your nose to fill your lungs completely.
exercises.tip: Breathe out slowly through your mouth. Make your breath out twice as long as your breath in. Do 3 to 5 times.

exercises.name: Doorway Pec Stretch
exercises.start: Stand in a doorway.
exercises.sub: Counteracts internal shoulder rotation from carrying bags

steps.label: Doorway Setup
steps.cue: Stand in a doorway. Place both forearms on the door frame at 90° — elbows at shoulder height, upper arms parallel to floor. Step one foot forward.

steps.label: Lean Through
steps.cue: Gently shift your weight forward through the doorway. Let the chest open. You should feel a stretch across the pectorals and front of the shoulders.

steps.label: Hold & Breathe

exercises.position: Stand in a doorway.
exercises.next: Place both forearms on the door frame. Keep your elbows bent at shoulder height. Step one foot forward.
exercises.then: Lean your chest gently forward through the doorway.
exercises.tip: Hold for 30 to 45 seconds. Keep your shoulders relaxed down.

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
exercises.start: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
exercises.sub: Re-engages the posterior chain after hours of sitting

steps.label: Supine Setup
steps.cue: Lie on your back. Feet flat, hip-width, heels 6 inches from glutes. Arms flat at sides. This is your reset position after travel.

steps.label: Drive & Lift
steps.cue: Press through the heels and lift the hips until shoulders, hips, and knees form a straight line. Squeeze the glutes firmly at the top. Hold 2 seconds.

steps.label: Lower Slowly
steps.cue: Lower with control — 3 seconds down. Do not let the hips crash. The slow descent builds the posterior chain that sitting disengages.

exercises.position: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
exercises.next: Keep your feet hip-width apart. Rest your arms flat at your sides.
exercises.then: Push your heels into the floor. Lift your hips until your body makes a straight line from your shoulders to your knees. Squeeze your butt at the top.
exercises.tip: Lower your hips slowly back to the floor. Take 3 seconds to go down.

walking.label: Walk Ease
walking.subtitle: Pre-Walk, Post-Walk, Reset
walking.purpose: Wake the gait system before the miles, then decompress calves, quads, and arches after.

sections.label: Pre-Walk: The Gait Prep
sections.purpose: Wake ankles, hips, and arms before a long walk.

exercises.name: Ankle Circles
exercises.start: Stand tall. Rest one hand on a wall for balance.
exercises.sub: Mobilize the ankles before the miles.

steps.cue: Lift one foot off the ground while lightly supporting yourself on the wall if needed.
Rotate the ankle in slow, controlled clockwise circles.
Switch to counter-clockwise circles, then repeat with the other foot.

exercises.position: Stand tall. Rest one hand on a wall for balance.
exercises.next: Lift one foot slightly off the floor.
exercises.then: Move your ankle in slow circles to the right.
exercises.tip: Move your ankle in circles to the left. Switch feet.

exercises.name: Glute Activation Drive
exercises.start: Stand tall with your feet apart.
exercises.sub: Fire the glutes to support the pelvis with each step.

steps.cue: Shift weight to your left leg and engage your core.
Lift the right knee toward your chest while squeezing the left glute.
Lower the foot with control and repeat on the other side, staying tall.

exercises.position: Stand tall with your feet apart.
exercises.next: Shift your weight onto your left leg. Squeeze your stomach muscles.
exercises.then: Lift your right knee up toward your chest. Squeeze your left butt muscle firmly.
exercises.tip: Lower your right foot slowly. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Controlled Arm Swings
exercises.start: Stand tall with your shoulders relaxed down.
exercises.sub: Rehearse efficient arm swing for walking.

steps.cue: Bend your elbows slightly and let the arms hang by your sides.
Swing your arms forward and backward from the shoulder joint in a natural rhythm.
Keep your neck long and avoid shrugging the shoulders as you move.

exercises.position: Stand tall with your shoulders relaxed down.
exercises.next: Bend your elbows slightly. Let your arms hang at your sides.
exercises.then: Swing your arms forward and backward from your shoulders in a smooth rhythm.
exercises.tip: Keep your neck straight. Do not lift your shoulders up toward your ears.

sections.label: Post-Walk: The Decompression
sections.purpose: Release calves, quads, and foot arches after the miles.

exercises.name: Calf Release - Right Leg
exercises.start: Stand facing a wall.
exercises.sub: Lengthen the calf and Achilles after long walks.

steps.cue: Place both hands on the wall and step one foot back.
Keep the back heel on the floor and lean into the wall until you feel a stretch in the calf.
Slightly bend the back knee to target the lower calf and Achilles, then switch sides.

exercises.position: Stand facing a wall.
exercises.next: Place both hands flat on the wall. Step one foot back.
exercises.then: Keep your back heel flat on the floor. Lean your weight toward the wall until you feel a pull in your back calf.
exercises.tip: Bend your back knee slightly to stretch the lower part of your calf. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Calf Release - Left Leg
exercises.start: Stand facing a wall.
exercises.sub: Lengthen the calf and Achilles after long walks.

steps.cue: Place both hands on the wall and step one foot back.
Keep the back heel on the floor and lean into the wall until you feel a stretch in the calf.
Slightly bend the back knee to target the lower calf and Achilles, then switch sides.

exercises.position: Stand facing a wall.
exercises.next: Place both hands flat on the wall. Step one foot back.
exercises.then: Keep your back heel flat on the floor. Lean your weight toward the wall until you feel a pull in your back calf.
exercises.tip: Bend your back knee slightly to stretch the lower part of your calf. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Standing Quad Stretch - Right Leg
exercises.start: Stand tall. Hold onto a chair for balance.
exercises.sub: Open the front of the thigh after steady forward motion.

steps.cue: Reach back and grab your right ankle or foot.
Pull the heel toward your glute while keeping your knees together and torso upright.
Hold the stretch, then repeat on the left side.

exercises.position: Stand tall. Hold onto a chair for balance.
exercises.next: Keep your knees close together and stand up straight.
exercises.then: Bend your right knee and grab your right foot behind you. Pull your heel toward your butt.
exercises.tip: Keep your chest lifted. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Standing Quad Stretch - Left Leg
exercises.start: Stand tall. Hold onto a chair for balance.
exercises.sub: Open the front of the thigh after steady forward motion.

steps.cue: Reach back and grab your right ankle or foot.
Pull the heel toward your glute while keeping your knees together and torso upright.
Hold the stretch, then repeat on the left side.

exercises.position: Stand tall. Hold onto a chair for balance.
exercises.next: Keep your knees close together and stand up straight.
exercises.then: Bend your right knee and grab your right foot behind you. Pull your heel toward your butt.
exercises.tip: Keep your chest lifted. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Foot Arch Massage
exercises.start: Stand or sit in a chair.
exercises.sub: Reset the arches after time on your feet.

steps.cue: Place a small ball or firm object under the arch of your foot.
Apply gentle pressure and roll the foot forward and backward over the object.
Spend extra time on any tight or tender spots before switching feet.

exercises.position: Stand or sit in a chair.
exercises.next: Place a small ball under the bottom of your foot.
exercises.then: Press your foot down gently. Roll the ball forward and backward under your foot.
exercises.tip: Spend more time rolling over any spots that feel tight. Switch feet.

running.label: Run Reboot
running.subtitle: Impact, Hips, Recovery
running.purpose: Prime tendons and hips before impact, then restore structure and circulation post-run.

sections.label: Pre-Run: The Impact Ready
sections.purpose: Prime tendons, hips, and hamstrings before impact.

exercises.name: Pogo Hops
exercises.start: Stand tall with your feet apart.
exercises.sub: Wake up calf and Achilles for impact.

steps.cue: Keep your knees almost straight and stand tall.
Jump using only your ankles and calves, staying on the balls of your feet.
Maintain a quick, rhythmic pace to prime the tendons.

exercises.position: Stand tall with your feet apart.
exercises.next: Keep your knees straight. Stay light on your feet.
exercises.then: Hop up and down on the balls of your feet. Use only your calves and ankles to jump.
exercises.tip: Keep a fast, steady rhythm. Do not let your heels touch the floor.

exercises.name: Runner's Lunge with Reach - Right Side
exercises.start: Stand tall.
exercises.sub: Open hip flexors and side body before the run.

steps.cue: Step back into a lunge, keeping the back leg straight and front knee stacked over the ankle.
Reach both arms toward the ceiling and tilt your torso slightly away from the back leg.
Return to standing and switch sides with control.

exercises.position: Stand tall.
exercises.next: Step one foot far back into a lunge. Keep your back leg straight and your front knee bent over your ankle.
exercises.then: Reach both arms straight up to the ceiling. Lean your chest slightly away from your back leg.
exercises.tip: Step your feet back together. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Runner's Lunge with Reach - Left Side
exercises.start: Stand tall.
exercises.sub: Open hip flexors and side body before the run.

steps.cue: Step back into a lunge, keeping the back leg straight and front knee stacked over the ankle.
Reach both arms toward the ceiling and tilt your torso slightly away from the back leg.
Return to standing and switch sides with control.

exercises.position: Stand tall.
exercises.next: Step one foot far back into a lunge. Keep your back leg straight and your front knee bent over your ankle.
exercises.then: Reach both arms straight up to the ceiling. Lean your chest slightly away from your back leg.
exercises.tip: Step your feet back together. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Hamstring Scoops
exercises.start: Stand tall. Step one heel slightly forward.
exercises.sub: Lengthen the hamstrings and groove front-leg loading.

steps.cue: Hinge at the hips while keeping the front leg straight and back knee soft.
Reach down and scoop your hands toward the floor in front of the foot, then up overhead.
Take a small step forward and repeat on the other leg.

exercises.position: Stand tall. Step one heel slightly forward.
exercises.next: Point your front toes up to the ceiling. Keep your front leg straight and bend your back knee slightly.
exercises.then: Bend forward from your hips. Sweep your hands down toward your front foot, then reach them up to the ceiling as you stand.
exercises.tip: Take a small step forward and switch legs. Move in a smooth rhythm.

sections.label: Post-Run: The Structural Reset
sections.purpose: Open hips, front body, and assist venous return after the run.

exercises.name: Seated Figure-4
exercises.start: Sit on the floor with your legs straight out in front of you.
exercises.sub: Decompress the hips after repetitive strides.

steps.cue: Bend your left knee and cross your right ankle over the left thigh.
Lean forward slightly or pull the left thigh toward you to feel the stretch in the right hip.
Hold, then repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.position: Sit on the floor with your legs straight out in front of you.
exercises.next: Bend your left knee. Cross your right ankle over your left knee.
exercises.then: Lean your chest slightly forward or pull your left leg closer to your body.
exercises.tip: Sit tall. Keep your chest lifted. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Cobra Pose
exercises.start: Lie flat on your stomach.
exercises.sub: Restore extension through the front of the body.

steps.cue: Place your hands on the floor next to your shoulders with elbows close to your sides.
Gently press through your hands to lift your chest off the floor, keeping your hips grounded.
Draw shoulders away from your ears and breathe into the front of the body.

exercises.position: Lie flat on your stomach.
exercises.next: Place your hands on the floor next to your shoulders. Keep your elbows tucked in close to your sides.
exercises.then: Press your hands into the floor to lift your chest up. Keep your hips flat on the floor.
exercises.tip: Pull your shoulders down away from your ears. Breathe deeply.

exercises.name: Legs Up the Wall
exercises.start: Lie on your back. Move your hips as close to a wall as you can.
exercises.sub: Assist circulation and recovery after impact.

steps.cue: Extend your legs straight up against the wall with heels resting lightly.
Let your arms rest by your sides with palms up and jaw unclenched.
Stay here for about two minutes, breathing slowly to calm the system.

exercises.position: Lie on your back. Move your hips as close to a wall as you can.
exercises.next: Rest your legs straight up against the wall.
exercises.then: Rest your arms at your sides with your palms facing up. Relax your jaw.
exercises.tip: Breathe slowly. Stay in this position for two minutes.

golf.label: Swing Sync
golf.subtitle: Golf, Rotation, Ready
golf.purpose: Prepare rotation, wrists, and hips before the first tee, then decompress after the final putt.

sections.label: Pre-Game: The Dynamic Drive
sections.purpose: Prime rotation, wrists, and hips before stepping onto the first tee.

exercises.name: Standing Club Rotations
exercises.start: Stand tall with your feet wide.
exercises.sub: Separate upper body rotation from stable hips.

steps.cue: Hold a golf club across your upper back and shoulders, gripping each end. Keep your spine straight and knees slightly bent.
Rotate your torso to the right as far as comfortable while keeping your hips and feet facing forward.
Slowly rotate to the left, feeling the separation between your upper and lower body.

exercises.position: Stand tall with your feet wide.
exercises.next: Hold a golf club across your upper back with both hands. Bend your knees slightly. Keep your back straight.
exercises.then: Turn your chest to the right as far as you can. Keep your hips and feet pointing straight forward.
exercises.tip: Turn your chest slowly to the left.

exercises.name: Leg Swings
exercises.start: Stand tall near a wall or chair for balance.
exercises.sub: Lubricate the hip joint in front, back, and lateral planes.

steps.cue: Stand on one leg, engage your core, and rest a hand lightly on the wall or cart for balance.
Swing the opposite leg forward and backward in a controlled motion, gradually increasing the range as the hip warms up.
Switch to side-to-side swings in front of your body to open the hip socket in the lateral plane.

exercises.position: Stand tall near a wall or chair for balance.
exercises.next: Stand on one leg. Rest one hand lightly on the wall.
exercises.then: Swing your free leg forward and backward. Make the swings larger as your leg warms up.
exercises.tip: Turn sideways. Swing your leg left and right across the front of your body. Switch legs.

exercises.name: Active Wrist Circles
exercises.start: Stand tall and reach your arms straight out in front of you.
exercises.sub: Warm wrists and forearms for gripping the club.

steps.cue: Make light fists with both hands with arms extended at shoulder height.
Rotate your wrists in slow, controlled clockwise circles, using the full available range.
Change direction to counter-clockwise, releasing tension through the forearms.

exercises.position: Stand tall and reach your arms straight out in front of you.
exercises.next: Make loose fists with both hands. Keep your arms at shoulder height.
exercises.then: Move your fists in slow circles to the right. Make the circles as large as you can.
exercises.tip: Move your fists in slow circles to the left. Keep your elbows straight.

sections.label: Post-Game: The Fairway Recovery
sections.purpose: Decompress the spine, hips, and shoulders after a full round.

exercises.name: Open Books
exercises.start: Lie on your side with your knees bent and stacked.
exercises.sub: Restore thoracic rotation after repetitive swings.

steps.cue: Extend both arms straight out in front of you with palms touching and knees stacked.
Lift the top arm and rotate it slowly across your body toward the floor behind you while keeping your knees pinned together.
Breathe deeply into the stretch, then slowly return to the starting position and repeat.

exercises.position: Lie on your side with your knees bent and stacked.
exercises.next: Reach both arms straight out in front of you with your palms touching. Keep your knees glued together.
exercises.then: Lift your top arm. Reach it up and over your body toward the floor behind you. Follow your hand with your eyes.
exercises.tip: Take a deep breath. Slowly bring your arm back to the start. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Kneeling Hip Flexor Stretch
exercises.start: Kneel on the floor with your right knee down. Place your left foot flat in front of you.
exercises.sub: Lengthen the front of the hip after walking and swinging.

steps.cue: Keep your front foot flat, torso upright, and ribs stacked over the pelvis.
Gently tuck your pelvis under and lean forward slightly until you feel a stretch in the front of your back hip.
Reach the arm on the same side as your back knee toward the ceiling to deepen the release.

exercises.position: Kneel on the floor with your right knee down. Place your left foot flat in front of you.
exercises.next: Keep your chest up and your front foot flat on the floor.
exercises.then: Tuck your tailbone slightly under. Lean your hips gently forward until you feel a pull in your right thigh.
exercises.tip: Reach your right arm straight up to the ceiling to feel a deeper stretch. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Thread the Needle
exercises.start: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.
exercises.sub: Release tension between the shoulder blades.

steps.cue: Slide one arm underneath the other, palm up, reaching as far as comfortable.
Lower your shoulder and ear toward the floor, letting the upper back rotate.
Hold and breathe into the space between your shoulder blades before switching sides.

exercises.position: Start on all fours, hands and knees on the floor.
exercises.next: Place your hands under your shoulders and your knees under your hips.
exercises.then: Slide your right arm under your left arm. Reach as far as you can. Rest your right shoulder and head on the floor.
exercises.tip: Take deep breaths. Push back up and switch sides.

tennis.label: Court Prep
tennis.subtitle: Racket, Shoulders, Hips
tennis.purpose: Ready the lateral base and shoulders for court work, then release forearms, hips, and side body after play.

sections.label: Pre-Game: The Court Readiness
sections.purpose: Prepare hips, shoulders, and ankles for multi-directional court work.

exercises.name: Lateral Lunges with Reach - Right Side
exercises.start: Stand tall with your feet wide apart.
exercises.sub: Groove lateral load and balance for wide court coverage.

steps.cue: Shift your weight to the right, bending the right knee while keeping the left leg straight.
Reach your arms forward to balance your weight and keep the chest lifted.
Push off the right foot to return to center, then repeat on the left side.

exercises.position: Stand tall with your feet wide apart.
exercises.next: Keep your chest lifted.
exercises.then: Shift your weight to your right leg. Bend your right knee and keep your left leg straight. Reach your arms straight out in front of you.
exercises.tip: Push off your right foot to stand back up. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Lateral Lunges with Reach - Left Side
exercises.start: Stand tall with your feet wide apart.
exercises.sub: Groove lateral load and balance for wide court coverage.

steps.cue: Shift your weight to the right, bending the right knee while keeping the left leg straight.
Reach your arms forward to balance your weight and keep the chest lifted.
Push off the right foot to return to center, then repeat on the left side.

exercises.position: Stand tall with your feet wide apart.
exercises.next: Keep your chest lifted.
exercises.then: Shift your weight to your right leg. Bend your right knee and keep your left leg straight. Reach your arms straight out in front of you.
exercises.tip: Push off your right foot to stand back up. Switch sides.

exercises.name: Shoulder Y & T Raises
exercises.start: Stand tall and lean slightly forward from your hips.
exercises.sub: Activate lower traps and posterior shoulder for overhead work.

steps.cue: Let your arms hang down with thumbs pointing out and spine long.
Raise your arms up to form a Y shape, squeezing the shoulder blades together.
Lower your arms, then raise them straight out to the sides to form a T, maintaining control.

exercises.position: Stand tall and lean slightly forward from your hips.
exercises.next: Let your arms hang straight down. Point your thumbs up toward the ceiling. Keep your back straight.
exercises.then: Lift your arms straight up and out to make a Y shape. Squeeze your shoulder blades together.
exercises.tip: Lower your arms. Lift your arms straight out to the sides to make a T shape.

exercises.name: Ankle Hops
exercises.start: Stand tall with your feet close together.
exercises.sub: Prime calf and Achilles for quick first steps.

steps.cue: Stay light on the balls of your feet with knees slightly soft.
Perform small, quick vertical hops, focusing on a fast bounce off the ground.
Keep your core tight and land softly each time without letting the heels slam down.

exercises.position: Stand tall with your feet close together.
exercises.next: Keep your knees slightly bent. Stay light on the balls of your feet.
exercises.then: Hop up and down quickly. Bounce fast off the floor.
exercises.tip: Keep your stomach muscles tight. Do not let your heels hit the floor.

sections.label: Post-Game: The Decompression
sections.purpose: Release forearms, hips, and side body after play.

exercises.name: Forearm Extensor Release - Right Side
exercises.start: Stand or sit. Reach one arm straight out in front of you.
exercises.sub: Unload the outer elbow and backhand tissues.

steps.cue: Point your fingers toward the floor with the palm facing you.
Use the opposite hand to gently pull the back of your hand toward your body until you feel a stretch.
Hold for several breaths, then ease off and switch sides.

exercises.position: Stand or sit. Reach one arm straight out in front of you.
exercises.next: Point your fingers down toward the floor. Turn your palm to face your body.
exercises.then: Use your other hand to gently pull the back of your hand toward your body.
exercises.tip: Hold the stretch. Let go slowly and switch arms.

exercises.name: Forearm Extensor Release - Left Side
exercises.start: Stand or sit. Reach one arm straight out in front of you.
exercises.sub: Unload the outer elbow and backhand tissues.

steps.cue: Point your fingers toward the floor with the palm facing you.
Use the opposite hand to gently pull the back of your hand toward your body until you feel a stretch.
Hold for several breaths, then ease off and switch sides.

exercises.position: Stand or sit. Reach one arm straight out in front of you.
exercises.next: Point your fingers down toward the floor. Turn your palm to face your body.
exercises.then: Use your other hand to gently pull the back of your hand toward your body.
exercises.tip: Hold the stretch. Let go slowly and switch arms.

exercises.name: Figure-4 Glute Stretch
exercises.start: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
exercises.sub: Open the hips after repeated lunges and changes of direction.

steps.cue: Cross your right ankle over your left knee to create a figure-4 shape.
Reach through and grab your left thigh, gently pulling it toward your chest.
Hold for several breaths, then repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.position: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
